t.sahih-int: And [mention] the Day when the heaven will split open with [emerging] clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent
t.asad: And on the Day on which the skies, together with the clouds, shall burst asunder, and the angels are made to descend in a mighty descent –
t.yusuf-ali: The Day the heaven shall be rent asunder with clouds, and angels shall be sent down, descending (in ranks)
t.pickthall: A day when the heaven with the clouds will be rent asunder and the angels will be sent down, a grand descent
t.arberry: Upon the day that heaven is split asunder with the clouds and the angels are sent down in majesty
t.hilali-khan: And (remember) the Day when the heaven shall be rent asunder with clouds, and the angels will be sent down, with a grand descending
t.itani: The Day when the sky is cleft with clouds, and the angels are sent down in streams.
t.mubarakpuri: And (remember) the Day when the heaven shall be rent asunder with clouds, and the angels will be sent down, with a grand descending.
t.qarib: The Day when heaven will split open with clouds [emerging], and the angels are sent down successively in streams
t.wahiduddin: On a Day when the sky will split open with its clouds and the angels are sent down rank upon rank
t.shakir: And on the day when the heaven shall burst asunder with the clouds, and the angels shall be sent down descending (in ranks)
t.haleem: On the Day when the sky and its clouds are split apart and the angels sent down in streams
t.maariful: The Day the sky will break open with a cloud, and the angels will be sent down in a majestic descent
t.khattab: ˹Watch for˺ the Day the heavens will burst with clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive ranks
t.ghali: And the Day that the heaven is cloven with the mist and the Angels are sent down, a (successive) sending down
t.irving: on a day when the sky will split open along with its clouds, and the angels will be sent right on down
t.iwq: And mention the Day when the heaven will split open with emerging clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent

t_id.kemenag: Dan (ingatlah) hari (ketika) langit pecah belah mengeluarkan kabut putih dan diturunkanlah malaikat bergelombang-gelombang.
t_id.sabiq: Ingatlah, wahai Nabi, hari ketika langit pecah dan terbuka. Kemudian dari celah-celahnya keluar kabut putih dan secara pasti malaikat turun.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ingatlah di hari ketika langit pecah) yaitu semua langit (mengeluarkan kabut) seraya mengeluarkan kabut yang berwarna putih (dan diturunkan Malaikat) dari setiap lapisan langit (bergelombang-gelombang) pada hari kiamat itu. Dinashabkannya lafal Yauma karena pada sebelumnya diperkirakan ada lafal Udzkur. Menurut qiraat yang lain lafal Tasyaqqaqu dibaca Tasysyaqqaqu dengan ditasydidkannya huruf Syin yang diambil dari asal kata Tatasyaqqaqu. Kemudian huruf Ta yang kedua diganti menjadi Syin lalu diidgamkan kepada Syin yang kedua sehingga menjadi Tasysyaqqaqu. Sedangkan menurut qiraat yang lainnya lagi lafal Nuzzila dibaca Nunzilu dan lafal Al Malaaikatu dibaca Al Malaaikata, sehingga bacaan lengkapnya menurut qiraat ini menjadi Nunzilul Malaaikata, artinya, Kami menurunkan Malaikat-malaikat.
t_id.iwq: Dan (ingatlah) hari (ketika) langit pecah belah mengeluarkan kabut putih dan diturunkanlah malaikat bergelombang-gelombang.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ويوم تشقق السماء» أي كل سماء «بالغمام» أي معه وهو غيم أبيض «ونزّل الملائكة» من كل سماء «تنزيلا» هو يوم القيامة ونصبه باذكر مقدرا وفي قراءة بتشديد شين تشقق بإدغام التاء الثانية في الأصل فيها، وفي أخرى: ننزل بنونين الثانية ساكنة وضم اللام ونصب الملائكة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَيَوۡمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِٱلۡغَمَٰمِ وَنُزِّلَ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ تَنزِيلًا (بِالْغَمَامِ: بِالسَّحَابِ الأَبْيَضِ الرَّقِيقِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ingatlah di hari ketika langit pecah) yaitu semua langit (mengeluarkan kabut) seraya mengeluarkan kabut yang berwarna putih (dan diturunkan Malaikat) dari setiap lapisan langit (bergelombang-gelombang) pada hari kiamat itu. Dinashabkannya lafal Yauma karena pada sebelumnya diperkirakan ada lafal Udzkur. Menurut qiraat yang lain lafal Tasyaqqaqu dibaca Tasysyaqqaqu dengan ditasydidkannya huruf Syin yang diambil dari asal kata Tatasyaqqaqu. Kemudian huruf Ta yang kedua diganti menjadi Syin lalu diidgamkan kepada Syin yang kedua sehingga menjadi Tasysyaqqaqu. Sedangkan menurut qiraat yang lainnya lagi lafal Nuzzila dibaca Nunzilu dan lafal Al Malaaikatu dibaca Al Malaaikata, sehingga bacaan lengkapnya menurut qiraat ini menjadi Nunzilul Malaaikata, artinya, Kami menurunkan Malaikat-malaikat

ar: ٱلۡمُلۡكُ یَوۡمَئِذٍ ٱلۡحَقُّ لِلرَّحۡمَـٰنِۚ وَكَانَ یَوۡمًا عَلَى ٱلۡكَـٰفِرِینَ عَسِیرࣰا
ar_simple: الْمُلْكُ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْحَقُّ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ ۚ وَكَانَ يَوْمًا عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ عَسِيرًا

t.sahih-int: True sovereignty, that Day, is for the Most Merciful. And it will be upon the disbelievers a difficult Day
t.asad: on that Day [it will become obvious to all that] true sovereignty belongs to the Most Gracious [alone]: hence, a Day of distress will it be for all who deny the truth
t.yusuf-ali: That Day, the dominion as of right and truth, shall be (wholly) for (Allah) Most Merciful: it will be a Day of dire difficulty for the misbelievers
t.pickthall: The Sovereignty on that day will be the True (Sovereignty) belonging to the Beneficent One, and it will be a hard day for disbelievers
t.arberry: the Kingdom that day, the true Kingdom, shall belong to the All-merciful and it shall be a day harsh for the unbelievers
t.hilali-khan: The sovereignty on that Day will be the true (sovereignty), belonging to the Most Beneficent (Allah), and it will be a hard Day for the disbelievers (those who disbelieve in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism)
t.itani: On that Day, true sovereignty will belong to the Merciful, and it will be a difficult Day for the disbelievers.
t.mubarakpuri: The sovereignty on that Day will be the true (sovereignty) of the Most Gracious, and it will be a hard Day for the disbelievers.
t.qarib: True sovereignty, on that Day, will belong to the Merciful-to-all. For unbelievers, it will be a day of distress
t.wahiduddin: true sovereignty on that Day will belong to the Gracious One, and it will be a hard Day for those who deny the truth
t.shakir: The kingdom on that day shall rightly belong to the Beneficent Allah, and a hard day shall it be for the unbelievers
t.haleem: on that Day, true authority belongs to the Lord of Mercy. It will be a grievous Day for the disbelievers
t.maariful: the Kingdom on that day will be for the RaHmān (the All-Merciful, Allah), and it will be a difficult day for the disbelievers
t.khattab: True authority on that Day will belong ˹only˺ to the Most Compassionate. And it will be a hard day for the disbelievers
t.ghali: The Kingdom, upon that Day, the true (Kingdom), will belong to The All-Merciful, and it will ever be a Day difficult for the disbelievers
t.irving: True control will belong to the Mercy-giving on that day. It will be a harsh day for disbelievers
t.iwq: True sovereignty, that Day, is for the Ever-Merciful. And it will be upon the disbelievers a difficult Day

t_id.kemenag: Kerajaan yang hak pada hari itu adalah kepunyaan Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah. Dan adalah (hari itu), satu hari penuh kesukaran bagi orang-orang kafir.
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari itu kepemilikan dan pengakuan manusia menjadi terputus. Kepemilikan saat itu hanya berada di tangan Allah. Hari itu menjadi hari yang sangat sulit bagi orang-orang kafir.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kerajaan yang hak pada hari itu adalah kepunyaan Allah Yang Maha Pemurah) tiada seorang pun yang menyaingi-Nya dalam hal ini. (Dan adalah) hari itu (satu hari yang penuh kesukaran bagi orang-orang kafir) berbeda dengan keadaan orang-orang yang beriman.
t_id.iwq: Kerajaan yang hak pada hari itu adalah kepunyaan Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah. Dan adalah (hari itu), satu hari penuh kesukaran bagi orang-orang kafir.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «الملك يومئذ الحق للرحمن» لا يشركه فيه أحد «وكان» اليوم «يوما على الكافرين عسيرا» بخلاف المؤمنين
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱلۡمُلۡكُ يَوۡمَئِذٍ ٱلۡحَقُّ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِۚ وَكَانَ يَوۡمًا عَلَى ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ عَسِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kerajaan yang hak pada hari itu adalah kepunyaan Allah Yang Maha Pemurah) tiada seorang pun yang menyaingi-Nya dalam hal ini. (Dan adalah) hari itu (satu hari yang penuh kesukaran bagi orang-orang kafir) berbeda dengan keadaan orang-orang yang beriman

ar: وَیَوۡمَ یَعَضُّ ٱلظَّالِمُ عَلَىٰ یَدَیۡهِ یَقُولُ یَـٰلَیۡتَنِی ٱتَّخَذۡتُ مَعَ ٱلرَّسُولِ سَبِیلࣰا
ar_simple: وَيَوْمَ يَعَضُّ الظَّالِمُ عَلَىٰ يَدَيْهِ يَقُولُ يَا لَيْتَنِي اتَّخَذْتُ مَعَ الرَّسُولِ سَبِيلًا

t.sahih-int: And the Day the wrongdoer will bite on his hands [in regret] he will say, "Oh, I wish I had taken with the Messenger a way
t.asad: and a Day on which the evildoer will bite his hands [in despair], exclaiming: “Oh, would that I had followed the path shown to me by the apostle
t.yusuf-ali: The Day that the wrong-doer will bite at his hands, he will say, "Oh! would that I had taken a (straight) path with the Messenger
t.pickthall: On the day when the wrong-doer gnaweth his hands, he will say: Ah, would that I had chosen a way together with the messenger (of Allah)
t.arberry: Upon the day the evildoer shall bite his hands, saying, 'Would that I had taken a way along with the Messenger
t.hilali-khan: And (remember) the Day when the Zalim (wrong-doer, oppressor, polytheist, etc.) will bite at his hands, he will say: "Oh! Would that I had taken a path with the Messenger (Muhammad SAW)
t.itani: On that Day, the wrongdoer will bite his hands, and say, “If only I had followed the way with the Messenger.
t.mubarakpuri: And (remember) the Day when the wrongdoer will bite at his hands, he will say: "Oh! Would that I had taken a path with the Messenger."
t.qarib: on that Day the one who was unjust to himself will bite his hands [in regret] and will say, "How I wish I had followed the way of the Messenger
t.wahiduddin: On that Day, the wrongdoer will bite his hands and say, "Would that I had walked in the Messenger's path
t.shakir: And the day when the unjust one shall bite his hands saying: O! would that I had taken a way with the Apostle
t.haleem: On that Day the evildoer will bite his own hand and say, ‘If only I had taken the same path as the Messenger
t.maariful: And (Be mindful of) the Day the wrongdoer will bite his hands saying, “Would that I had taken a path along with the messenger
t.khattab: And ˹beware of˺ the Day the wrongdoer will bite his nails ˹in regret˺ and say, “Oh! I wish I had followed the Way along with the Messenger
t.ghali: And the Day that the unjust (person) will bite at both his hands, (and) say, "Oh, would that I had taken to myself a way along with the Messenger
t.irving: a day when the wrongdoer will gnaw away at his hands
t.iwq: And the Day the wrongdoer will bite on his hands in regret he will say, "Oh, I wish I had taken with the Messenger a way

t_id.kemenag: Dan (ingatlah) hari (ketika itu) orang yang zalim menggigit dua tangannya, seraya berkata: "Aduhai kiranya (dulu) aku mengambil jalan bersama-sama Rasul".
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari kiamat, orang yang menzalimi dirinya dengan kekafiran dan melanggar para rasul menggigit kedua tangannya dengan penuh penyesalan. Dengan berangan-angan mereka berkata, "Aduhai kiranya dulu aku mengikuti para rasul sehingga aku menelusuri jalan menuju surga dan menjauhi jalan menuju neraka."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ingatlah hari ketika itu orang yang zalim) orang musyrik, yaitu Uqbah bin Mu'ith yang pernah membaca dua kalimat syahadat, kemudian ia menjadi murtad demi mengambil hati Ubay bin Khalaf (menggigit dua tangannya) karena menyesal dan kecewa, di hari kiamat (seraya berkata, "Aduhai!) huruf Ya menunjukkan makna penyesalan (Kiranya dahulu aku mengambil bersama Rasul) yakni Nabi Muhammad (jalan) petunjuk.
t_id.iwq: Dan (ingatlah) hari (ketika itu) orang yang zalim menggigit doa tangannya, seraya berkata: "Aduhai kiranya (dulu) aku mengambil jalan bersama-sama Rasul".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ويوم يعضُّ الظالم» المشرك: عقبة بن أبي معيط كان نطق بالشهادتين ثم رجع إرضاءً لأبيّ بن خلف «على يديه» ندما وتحسرا في يوم القيامة «يقول يا» للتنبيه «ليتني اتخذت مع الرسول» محمد «سبيلا» طريقا إلى الهدى
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَيَوۡمَ يَعَضُّ ٱلظَّالِمُ عَلَىٰ يَدَيۡهِ يَقُولُ يَٰلَيۡتَنِي ٱتَّخَذۡتُ مَعَ ٱلرَّسُولِ سَبِيلٗا (سَبِيلًا: طَرِيقًا إِلَى الجَنَّةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ingatlah hari ketika itu orang yang zalim) orang musyrik, yaitu Uqbah bin Mu'ith yang pernah membaca dua kalimat syahadat, kemudian ia menjadi murtad demi mengambil hati Ubay bin Khalaf (menggigit dua tangannya) karena menyesal dan kecewa, di hari kiamat (seraya berkata, "Aduhai!) huruf Ya menunjukkan makna penyesalan (Kiranya dahulu aku mengambil bersama Rasul) yakni Nabi Muhammad (jalan) petunjuk

ar: یَـٰوَیۡلَتَىٰ لَیۡتَنِی لَمۡ أَتَّخِذۡ فُلَانًا خَلِیلࣰا
ar_simple: يَا وَيْلَتَىٰ لَيْتَنِي لَمْ أَتَّخِذْ فُلَانًا خَلِيلًا

t.sahih-int: Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend
t.asad: Oh, woe is me! Would that I had not taken so-and-so for a friend
t.yusuf-ali: Ah! woe is me! Would that I had never taken such a one for a friend
t.pickthall: Alas for me! Ah, would that I had never taken such an one for friend
t.arberry: Alas, would that I had not taken So-and-so for a friend
t.hilali-khan: Ah! Woe to me! Would that I had never taken so-and-so as a friend
t.itani: Oh, woe to me; I wish I never took so-and-so for a friend.
t.mubarakpuri: "Ah! Woe to me! Would that I had never taken so-and-so as an intimate friend!"
t.qarib: Woe to me! I wish I had not taken so and so as a friend
t.wahiduddin: Oh, would that I had never chosen such a one for my companion
t.shakir: O woe is me! would that I had not taken such a one for a friend
t.haleem: Woe is me! If only I had not taken so and so as a friend
t.maariful: Woe to me! Would that I had not taken so-and-so for my friend
t.khattab: Woe to me! I wish I had never taken so-and-so as a close friend
t.ghali: Oh, woe to me! Would that I had not taken so-and-so to myself for a (close) fellow
t.irving: He will say: "It´s too bad for me! If I had only taken a pathway alongside the Messenger! It´s too bad for me! If I had not adopted So-and-So as a close friend
t.iwq: Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend

t_id.kemenag: Kecelakaan besarlah bagiku; kiranya aku (dulu) tidak menjadikan sifulan itu teman akrab(ku).
t_id.sabiq: Dengan nada menyesal karena mengikuti orang-orang yang menyesatkan, mereka berkata, "Aduhai kiranya aku tidak menjadikan Polan sebagai teman yang Engkau jadikan pemimpinku.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kecelakaan besar bagiku) huruf Alif dari lafal Yaa Wailataa merupakan pergantian Ya Idhafah, asalnya adalah Yaa Wailatii maknanya alangkah binasanya aku (kiranya aku dahulu tidak menjadikan si Polan itu) yakni Ubay bin Khalaf yang dijilatnya tadi (teman akrab).
t_id.iwq: Kecelakaan besarlah bagiku; kiranya aku (dulu) tidak menjadikan sifulan itu teman akrab(ku).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا وَيْلَتَى» ألفه عوض عن ياء الإضافة أي ويلتي، ومعناه هلكتي «ليتني لم أتخذ فلانا» أي أبيَّا «خليلا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَٰوَيۡلَتَىٰ لَيۡتَنِي لَمۡ أَتَّخِذۡ فُلَانًا خَلِيلٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kecelakaan besar bagiku) huruf Alif dari lafal Yaa Wailataa merupakan pergantian Ya Idhafah, asalnya adalah Yaa Wailatii maknanya alangkah binasanya aku (kiranya aku dahulu tidak menjadikan si Polan itu) yakni Ubay bin Khalaf yang dijilatnya tadi (teman akrab)

ar: لَّقَدۡ أَضَلَّنِی عَنِ ٱلذِّكۡرِ بَعۡدَ إِذۡ جَاۤءَنِیۗ وَكَانَ ٱلشَّیۡطَـٰنُ لِلۡإِنسَـٰنِ خَذُولࣰا
ar_simple: لَقَدْ أَضَلَّنِي عَنِ الذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ جَاءَنِي ۗ وَكَانَ الشَّيْطَانُ لِلْإِنْسَانِ خَذُولًا

t.sahih-int: He led me away from the remembrance after it had come to me. And ever is Satan, to man, a deserter
t.asad: Indeed, he led me astray from the remembrance [of God] after it had come unto me!” For [thus it is:] Satan is ever a betrayer of man
t.yusuf-ali: He did lead me astray from the Message (of Allah) after it had come to me! Ah! the Evil One is but a traitor to man
t.pickthall: He verily led me astray from the Reminder after it had reached me. Satan was ever man's deserter in the hour of need
t.arberry: He indeed led me astray from the Remembrance, after it had come to me; Satan is ever a forsaker of men
t.hilali-khan: He indeed led me astray from the Reminder (this Quran) after it had come to me. And Shaitan (Satan) is ever a deserter to man in the hour of need
t.itani: He led me away from the Message after it had come to me; for Satan has always been a betrayer of man.”
t.mubarakpuri: "He indeed led me astray from the Reminder after it had come to me. And Shaytan is to man ever a deserter."
t.qarib: He led me away from the remembrance [of God] after it had come to me. Satan, has always let man down
t.wahiduddin: he made me forgetful of the warning after it had reached me. Satan is man's great betrayer
t.shakir: Certainly he led me astray from the reminder after it had come to me; and the Shaitan fails to aid man
t.haleem: he led me away from the Revelation after it reached me. Satan has always betrayed mankind.’
t.maariful: Indeed he led me astray from the advice after it had come to me.” And the Satan is man’s betrayer
t.khattab: It was he who truly made me stray from the Reminder after it had reached me.” And Satan has always betrayed humanity
t.ghali: Indeed he readily made me err away from the Remembrance after it had come to me." And Ash-Shaytan (i.e., dwelling habitation) has been constantly abandoning man
t.irving: He led me astray from the Reminder even after it had reached me. Satan is such a turncoat with man
t.iwq: And he led me away from the remembrance after it had come to me. And ever is Satan, to man, a deserter

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya dia telah menyesatkan aku dari Al Quran ketika Al Quran itu telah datang kepadaku. Dan adalah syaitan itu tidak mau menolong manusia.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya si Polan itu telah menjauhkan kami dari mengingat Allah dan al-Qur'ân, padahal hal itu sangat mudah bagi kami." Demikianlah setan menaklukkan manusia dan menjerumuskannya ke jurang kehancuran.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya dia telah menyesatkan aku dari peringatan) Alquran (sesudah peringatan itu datang kepadaku") karena dialah yang menjadikan aku murtad dan tidak beriman lagi kepada Alquran. Kemudian Allah berfirman, ("Dan adalah setan itu terhadap manusia) yang kafir (selalu membuat kecewa.") karena ia akan meninggalkannya begitu saja, cuci tangan bilamana manusia tertimpa malapetaka.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya dia telah menyesatkan aku dari Al Al-Quran ketika Al Al-Quran itu telah datang kepadaku. Dan adalah syaitan itu tidak mau menolong manusia.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «لقد أضلني عن الذكر» أي القرآن «بعد إذ جاءني» بأن ردَّني عن الإيمان به، قال تعالى: «وكان الشيطان للإنسان» الكافر «خذولا» بأن يتركه ويتبرأ منه عند البلاء
tafsir.siraj-ar: لَّقَدۡ أَضَلَّنِي عَنِ ٱلذِّكۡرِ بَعۡدَ إِذۡ جَآءَنِيۗ وَكَانَ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُ لِلۡإِنسَٰنِ خَذُولٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya dia telah menyesatkan aku dari peringatan) Alquran (sesudah peringatan itu datang kepadaku") karena dialah yang menjadikan aku murtad dan tidak beriman lagi kepada Alquran. Kemudian Allah berfirman, ("Dan adalah setan itu terhadap manusia) yang kafir (selalu membuat kecewa.") karena ia akan meninggalkannya begitu saja, cuci tangan bilamana manusia tertimpa malapetaka

ar: وَقَالَ ٱلرَّسُولُ یَـٰرَبِّ إِنَّ قَوۡمِی ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ هَـٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ مَهۡجُورࣰا
ar_simple: وَقَالَ الرَّسُولُ يَا رَبِّ إِنَّ قَوْمِي اتَّخَذُوا هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنَ مَهْجُورًا

t.sahih-int: And the Messenger has said, "O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Qur'an as [a thing] abandoned
t.asad: AND [on that Day] the Apostle will say: “O my Sustainer! Behold, [some of] my people have come to regard this Qur’an as something [that ought to be] discarded!”
t.yusuf-ali: Then the Messenger will say: "O my Lord! Truly my people took this Qur'an for just foolish nonsense
t.pickthall: And the messenger saith: O my Lord! Lo! mine own folk make this Qur'an of no account
t.arberry: The Messenger says, 'O my Lord, behold, my people have taken this Koran as a thing to be shunned
t.hilali-khan: And the Messenger (Muhammad SAW) will say: "O my Lord! Verily, my people deserted this Quran (neither listened to it, nor acted on its laws and orders)
t.itani: And the Messenger will say, “My Lord, my people have abandoned this Quran.”
t.mubarakpuri: And the Messenger will say: "O my Lord! Verily, my people deserted this Qur'an."
t.qarib: The Messenger will say, "My Lord, This Qur'an was abandoned by my people
t.wahiduddin: The Messenger will say, "Lord, my people did indeed discard the Quran
t.shakir: And the Apostle cried out: O my Lord! surely my people have treated this Quran as a forsaken thing
t.haleem: The Messenger has said, ‘Lord, my people treat this Quran as something to be shunned,’
t.maariful: And the messenger will say, “O my Lord, my people had taken this Qur’ān as deserted.”
t.khattab: The Messenger has cried, “O my Lord! My people have indeed received this Quran with neglect.”
t.ghali: And the Messenger has said, "O Lord! Surely my people have taken to themselves this Qur'an as a thing to be forsaken
t.irving: The Messenger will say: "My Lord, my folk assume this Reading is out-of-date
t.iwq: And the Messenger has said, "O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Qur'an as a thing abandoned

t_id.kemenag: Berkatalah Rasul: "Ya Tuhanku, sesungguhnya kaumku menjadikan Al Quran itu sesuatu yang tidak diacuhkan".
t_id.sabiq: Rasulullah mengadukan kesombongan kaumnya yang dia rasakan kepada Allah dengan mengatakan, "Sesungguhnya mereka telah meninggalkan al-Qur'ân dan mencampakkannya. Mereka juga makin menjadi dengan ketaksudian, kesombongan dan permusuhan yang ada pada mereka."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah Rasul) Nabi Muhammad, ("Ya Rabbku! Sesungguhnya kaumku) kabilah Quraisy (menjadikan Alquran ini suatu yang diasingkan") ditinggal begitu saja. Maka Allah swt. berfirman,
t_id.iwq: Berkatalah Rasul: "Ya Tuhanku, sesungguhnya kaumku menjadikan Al Al-Quran itu sesuatu yang tidak diacuhkan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقال الرسول» محمد «يا رب إن قومي» قريشا «اتخذوا هذا القرآن مهجورا» متروكا قال تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالَ ٱلرَّسُولُ يَٰرَبِّ إِنَّ قَوۡمِي ٱتَّخَذُواْ هَٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ مَهۡجُورٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah Rasul) Nabi Muhammad, ("Ya Rabbku! Sesungguhnya kaumku) kabilah Quraisy (menjadikan Alquran ini suatu yang diasingkan") ditinggal begitu saja. Maka Allah swt. berfirman

ar: وَكَذَ ٰلِكَ جَعَلۡنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِیٍّ عَدُوࣰّا مِّنَ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِینَۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ هَادِیࣰا وَنَصِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ عَدُوًّا مِنَ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ هَادِيًا وَنَصِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a guide and a helper
t.asad: For so it is that against every prophet We have set up enemies from among those who are lost in sin: yet none can guide and give succour as thy Sustainer does
t.yusuf-ali: Thus have We made for every prophet an enemy among the sinners: but enough is thy Lord to guide and to help
t.pickthall: Even so have We appointed unto every prophet an opponent from among the guilty; but Allah sufficeth for a Guide and Helper
t.arberry: Even so We have appointed to every Prophet an enemy among the sinners; but thy Lord suffices as a guide and as a helper
t.hilali-khan: Thus have We made for every Prophet an enemy among the Mujrimun (disbelievers, polytheists, criminals, etc.). But Sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper
t.itani: Likewise, to every prophet We assign enemies from among the wicked. But your Lord suffices as a Guide and Savior.
t.mubarakpuri: Thus have We made for every Prophet an enemy among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper.
t.qarib: and thus have We assigned to every prophet an enemy; those, who force others to reject Our messages. But your Lord is sufficient as a guide and a helper
t.wahiduddin: thus did We assign to every prophet an enemy from among the sinners; your Lord is sufficient as a guide and a helper
t.shakir: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the sinners and sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and a Helper
t.haleem: but We have always appointed adversaries from the wicked, for every prophet: Your Lord is sufficient guide and helper
t.maariful: In a similar way We made for every prophet an enemy from among the sinners, but your Lord suffices as a guide and as a supporter
t.khattab: Similarly, We made enemies for every prophet from among the wicked, but sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper
t.ghali: And thus We have made to every Prophet an enemy among the criminals; and your Lord suffices as a Guide and an Ever-Vindicator
t.irving: Thus have We granted every prophet an enemy among criminals. Yet your Lord suffices as a Guide and Supporter
t.iwq: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a guide and a helper

t_id.kemenag: Dan seperti itulah, telah Kami adakan bagi tiap-tiap nabi, musuh dari orang-orang yang berdosa. Dan cukuplah Tuhanmu menjadi Pemberi petunjuk dan Penolong.
t_id.sabiq: Seperti halnya kami menjadikan kaummu, hai Muhammad, memusuhi dan mendustakanmu, Kami menjadikan untuk setiap nabi seorang musuh dari para pelaku kejahatan yang memusuhi dan menentang seruannya. Allah akan menolongmu dan menunjuki untuk menaklukkan mereka. Cukuplah Allah sebagai pemberi petunjuk dan penolong.
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Dan seperti itulah) sebagaimana Kami telah menjadikan bagimu musuh dari kalangan orang-orang musyrik kaummu sendiri (Kami adakan bagi tiap-tiap Nabi) sebelum kamu (musuh dari orang-orang yang berdosa) yakni orang-orang musyrik maka bersabarlah sebagaimana mereka bersabar. (Dan cukuplah Rabbmu menjadi Pemberi petunjuk) bagimu (dan Penolong") yang menolong kamu terhadap musuh-musuhmu.
t_id.iwq: Dan seperti itulah, telah Kami adakan bagi tiap-tiap nabi, musuh dari orang-orang yang berdosa. Dan cukuplah Tuhanmu menjadi Pemberi petunjuk dan Penolong.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وكذلك» كما جعلنا لك عدوّا من مشركي قومك «جعلنا لكل نبي» قبلك «عدوا من المجرمين» المشركين فاصبر كما صبروا «وكفى بربك هاديا» لك «ونصيرا» ناصرا لك على أعدائك
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلۡنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ عَدُوّٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ هَادِيٗا وَنَصِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan seperti itulah) sebagaimana Kami telah menjadikan bagimu musuh dari kalangan orang-orang musyrik kaummu sendiri (Kami adakan bagi tiap-tiap Nabi) sebelum kamu (musuh dari orang-orang yang berdosa) yakni orang-orang musyrik maka bersabarlah sebagaimana mereka bersabar. (Dan cukuplah Rabbmu menjadi Pemberi petunjuk) bagimu (dan Penolong") yang menolong kamu terhadap musuh-musuhmu

ar: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِینَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوۡلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَیۡهِ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانُ جُمۡلَةࣰ وَ ٰحِدَةࣰۚ كَذَ ٰلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَۖ وَرَتَّلۡنَـٰهُ تَرۡتِیلࣰا
ar_simple: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ الْقُرْآنُ جُمْلَةً وَاحِدَةً ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِ فُؤَادَكَ ۖ وَرَتَّلْنَاهُ تَرْتِيلًا

t.sahih-int: And those who disbelieve say, "Why was the Qur'an not revealed to him all at once?" Thus [it is] that We may strengthen thereby your heart. And We have spaced it distinctly
t.asad: Now they who are bent on denying the truth are wont to ask. “Why has not the Qur’an been bestowed on him from on high in one single revelation?” [it has been revealed] in this manner so that We might strengthen thy heart thereby - for We have so arranged its component parts that they form one consistent whole
t.yusuf-ali: Those who reject Faith say: "Why is not the Qur'an revealed to him all at once? Thus (is it revealed), that We may strengthen thy heart thereby, and We have rehearsed it to thee in slow, well-arranged stages, gradually
t.pickthall: And those who disbelieve say: Why is the Qur'an not revealed unto him all at once? (It is revealed) thus that We may strengthen thy heart therewith; and We have arranged it in right order
t.arberry: The unbelievers say, 'Why has the Koran not been sent down upon him all at once?' Even so, that We may strengthen thy heart thereby, and We have chanted it very distinctly
t.hilali-khan: And those who disbelieve say: "Why is not the Quran revealed to him all at once?" Thus (it is sent down in parts), that We may strengthen your heart thereby. And We have revealed it to you gradually, in stages. (It was revealed to the Prophet SAW in 23 years)
t.itani: Those who disbelieve say, “Why was the Quran not revealed to him at once?” Thus in order to strengthen your heart thereby, and We revealed it in stages.
t.mubarakpuri: And those who disbelieve say: "Why is not the Qur'an revealed to him all at once" Thus, that We may strengthen your heart thereby. And We have revealed it to you gradually, in stages.
t.qarib: And the unbelievers say, "Why was the Qur'an not sent to him in one single revelation?" [It was revealed in stages] so that We may strengthen your heart. And We revealed it in well-organized portions
t.wahiduddin: Those who deny the truth say, "Why was the Quran not sent down to him in a single revelation?" We sent it in this manner, so that We might strengthen your heart. We gave it to you in gradual revelation
t.shakir: And those who disbelieve say: Why has not the Quran been revealed to him all at once? Thus, that We may strengthen your heart by it and We have arranged it well in arranging
t.haleem: The disbelievers also say, ‘Why was the Quran not sent down to him all at once?’ We sent it in this way to strengthen your heart [Prophet]; We gave it to you in gradual revelation
t.maariful: Said those who disbelieved, “Why has the Qur’ān not been revealed to him all at once?” (It has been sent down) in this way (i.e. in parts) so that We make your heart firm, and We revealed it little by little
t.khattab: The disbelievers say, “If only the Quran had been sent down to him all at once!” ˹We have sent it˺ as such ˹in stages˺ so We may reassure your heart with it. And We have revealed it at a deliberate pace
t.ghali: And the ones who have disbelieved have said, "If only the Qur'an had been sent down upon him as one complete whole." Thus, that We may make firm your heart-sight thereby, and We have recited it in a (distinct) recitation
t.irving: Those who disbelieve say: "Why has not the Quran been sent down to him in one single piece?" [It has been done] like that so your vitals may be braced by it; We have phrased it deliberately
t.iwq: And those who disbelieve say, "Why was the Qur'an not revealed to him all at once?" Thus it is that We may strengthen thereby your heart. And We have spaced it distinctly

t_id.kemenag: Berkatalah orang-orang yang kafir: "Mengapa Al Quran itu tidak diturunkan kepadanya sekali turun saja?"; demikianlah supaya Kami perkuat hatimu dengannya dan Kami membacanya secara tartil (teratur dan benar).
t_id.sabiq: Dengan maksud menghujat al-Qur'ân, orang-orang kafir berkata, " Kenapa al-Qur'ân tidak diturunkan sekaligus?" Sesungguhnya Kami menurunkan al-Qur'ân demikian, secara berangsur-angsur, agar hatimu menjadi teguh karena dapat menghafalnya. Kami membacakan al-Qur'ân melalui Jibril sedikit demi sedikit secara perlahan-lahan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah orang-orang yang kafir, "Mengapa tidak) (diturunkan kepadanya Alquran sekali turun saja?") sebagaimana kitab Taurat, kitab Injil dan kitab Zabur. Allah menjawab melalui firman-Nya, "Kami sengaja menurunkannya (demikian) secara terpisah-pisah (supaya Kami perkuat hatimu dengannya) Kami menguatkan kalbumu dengan Alquran (dan Kami membacakannya kelompok demi kelompok") Kami menurunkannya tahap demi tahap secara perlahan dan tidak tergesa-gesa, supaya mudah dipahami dan dihafal.
t_id.iwq: Berkatalah orang-orang yang kafir: "Mengapa Al Al-Quran itu tidak diturunkan kepadanya sekali turun saja?"; demikianlah supaya Kami perkuat hatimu dengannya dan Kami membacanya secara tartil (teratur dan benar).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقال الذين كفروا لولا» هلا «نزل عليه القرآن جملة واحدة» كالتوراة والإنجيل والزَّبور، قال تعالى: نزلناه، «كذلك» متفرّقا «لنثبَّت به فؤادك» نقوي قلبك «ورتلناه ترتيلا» أي أتينا به شيئا بعد شيء بتمهل وتؤدة لتيسر فهمه وحفظه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَوۡلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيۡهِ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانُ جُمۡلَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَۖ وَرَتَّلۡنَٰهُ تَرۡتِيلٗا (وَرَتَّلْنَاهُ: بَيَّنَّاهُ فِي تَثَبُّتٍ وَمُهْلَةٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah orang-orang yang kafir, "Mengapa tidak) (diturunkan kepadanya Alquran sekali turun saja?") sebagaimana kitab Taurat, kitab Injil dan kitab Zabur. Allah menjawab melalui firman-Nya, "Kami sengaja menurunkannya (demikian) secara terpisah-pisah (supaya Kami perkuat hatimu dengannya) Kami menguatkan kalbumu dengan Alquran (dan Kami membacakannya kelompok demi kelompok") Kami menurunkannya tahap demi tahap secara perlahan dan tidak tergesa-gesa, supaya mudah dipahami dan dihafal

ar: وَلَا یَأۡتُونَكَ بِمَثَلٍ إِلَّا جِئۡنَـٰكَ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَأَحۡسَنَ تَفۡسِیرًا
ar_simple: وَلَا يَأْتُونَكَ بِمَثَلٍ إِلَّا جِئْنَاكَ بِالْحَقِّ وَأَحْسَنَ تَفْسِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And they do not come to you with an argument except that We bring you the truth and the best explanation
t.asad: and [that] they [who deny the truth] might never taunt thee with any deceptive half-truth without Our conveying to thee the [full] truth and [providing thee] with the best explanation
t.yusuf-ali: And no question do they bring to thee but We reveal to thee the truth and the best explanation (thereof)
t.pickthall: And they bring thee no similitude but We bring thee the Truth (as against it), and better (than their similitude) as argument
t.arberry: They bring not to thee any similitude but that We bring thee the truth, and better in exposition
t.hilali-khan: And no example or similitude do they bring (to oppose or to find fault in you or in this Quran), but We reveal to you the truth (against that similitude or example), and the better explanation thereof
t.itani: Whatever argument they come to you with, We provide you with the truth, and a better exposition.
t.mubarakpuri: And no example or parable do they bring, but We reveal to you the truth, and the better explanation thereof.
t.qarib: And every time they come to you with an argument, We will give you the [full] Truth and the best explanation
t.wahiduddin: Every time they raise an objection, We will bring you the truth and the best of explanations
t.shakir: And they shall not bring to you any argument, but We have brought to you (one) with truth and best in significance
t.haleem: They cannot put any argument to you without Our bringing you the truth and the best explanation
t.maariful: They bring to you no hypothesis, but We bring to you the correct position and (an answer) better explained
t.khattab: Whenever they bring you an argument, We come to you with the right refutation and the best explanation
t.ghali: And they do not come up to you with any similitude, except that We come to you with the Truth and a fairer explanation
t.irving: They will not come to you with any example unless We will bring you the Truth and something even finer as a commentary
t.iwq: And they do not come to you with an argument except that We bring you the truth and the best explanation

t_id.kemenag: Tidaklah orang-orang kafir itu datang kepadamu (membawa) sesuatu yang ganjil, melainkan Kami datangkan kepadamu suatu yang benar dan yang paling baik penjelasannya.
t_id.sabiq: Setiap kali mereka mendatangkan kepadamu sanggahan-sanggahan yang tidak beralasan, Kami pasti mendatangkan kepadamu kebenaran yang kami jelaskan dengan sebaik-baiknya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tidaklah orang-orang kafir itu datang kepadamu membawa sesuatu yang ganjil) untuk membatalkan perkaramu (melainkan Kami datangkan kepadamu suatu yang benar) yang menolak dan membantahnya (dan yang paling baik penjelasannya) untuk menjelaskan perkara yang sebenarnya kepada mereka.
t_id.iwq: Tidaklah orang-orang kafir itu datang kepadamu (membawa) sesuatu yang ganjil, melainkan Kami datangkan kepadamu suatu yang benar dan yang paling baik penjelasannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولا يأتونك بمثل» في إبطال أمرك «إلا جئناك بالحق» الدافع له «وأحسن تفسيرا» بيانا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَا يَأۡتُونَكَ بِمَثَلٍ إِلَّا جِئۡنَٰكَ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَأَحۡسَنَ تَفۡسِيرًا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tidaklah orang-orang kafir itu datang kepadamu membawa sesuatu yang ganjil) untuk membatalkan perkaramu (melainkan Kami datangkan kepadamu suatu yang benar) yang menolak dan membantahnya (dan yang paling baik penjelasannya) untuk menjelaskan perkara yang sebenarnya kepada mereka

ar: ٱلَّذِینَ یُحۡشَرُونَ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمۡ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ أُو۟لَـٰۤئِكَ شَرࣱّ مَّكَانࣰا وَأَضَلُّ سَبِیلࣰا
ar_simple: الَّذِينَ يُحْشَرُونَ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمْ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ أُولَٰئِكَ شَرٌّ مَكَانًا وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا

t.sahih-int: The ones who are gathered on their faces to Hell - those are the worst in position and farthest astray in [their] way
t.asad: [And so, tell those who are bent on denying the truth that] they who shall be gathered unto hell upon their faces – it is they who [in the life to come] will be worst in station and still farther astray from the path [of truth]
t.yusuf-ali: Those who will be gathered to Hell (prone) on their faces,- they will be in an evil plight, and, as to Path, most astray
t.pickthall: Those who will be gathered on their faces unto hell: such are worse in plight and further from the right road
t.arberry: Those who shall be mustered to Gehenna upon their faces -- they shall be worse in place, and gone further astray from the way
t.hilali-khan: Those who will be gathered to Hell (prone) on their faces, such will be in an evil state, and most astray from the (Straight) Path
t.itani: Those who are herded into Hell on their faces—those are in a worse position, and further astray from the way.
t.mubarakpuri: Those who will be gathered to Hell on their faces, such will be in an evil state, and most astray from the path.
t.qarib: As for those who are dragged facedown to Hell, they will be in the worst place as they are the most misguided from the path
t.wahiduddin: Those who will be dragged headlong into Hell shall have an evil place to dwell in, for they have strayed far from the right path
t.shakir: (As for) those who shall be gathered upon their faces to hell, they are in a worse plight and straying farther away from the path
t.haleem: It is those driven [falling], on their faces,to Hell who will be in the worst place- they are the furthest from the right path
t.maariful: Those who will be driven on their faces to Hell - they are the worst in situation and far more astray from the path
t.khattab: Those who will be dragged into Hell on their faces will be in the worst place, and are ˹now˺ farthest from the ˹Right˺ Way
t.ghali: The ones who will be mustered to Hell upon their faces, those will be in an eviler place and will have (erred) further from the way
t.irving: Those who are dragged off face down to Hell will be in a worse plight and farthest off the track
t.iwq: The ones who are gathered on their faces to Hell - those are the worst in position and farthest astray in their way

t_id.kemenag: Orang-orang yang dihimpunkan ke neraka Jahannam dengan diseret atas muka-muka mereka, mereka itulah orang yang paling buruk tempatnya dan paling sesat jalannya.
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang yang mengingkari risalahmu akan diseret ke neraka dengan muka-muka yang menampakkan keterhinaan. Mereka adalah orang-orang yang mendapatkan tempat paling jelek dan orang-orang yang paling sesat jalannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: Mereka adalah (orang-orang yang dihimpunkan dengan diseret atas muka-muka mereka) mereka digiring seraya diseret (ke neraka Jahanam, mereka itulah orang-orang yang paling buruk tempatnya) yaitu neraka Jahanam (dan paling sesat jalannya) paling keliru jalannya bila dibandingkan dengan selain mereka, sebab kekafirannya.
t_id.iwq: Orang-orang yang dihimpunkan ke neraka neraka dengan diseret atas muka-muka mereka, mereka itulah orang yang paling buruk tempatnya dan paling sesat jalannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: هم «الذين يُحشرون على وجوههم» أي يساقون «إلى جهنم أولئك شرٌ مكانا» هو جهنم «وأضَلُّ سبيلا» أخطأ طريقا من غيرهم وهو كفرهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱلَّذِينَ يُحۡشَرُونَ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمۡ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ أُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ شَرّٞ مَّكَانٗا وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Mereka adalah (orang-orang yang dihimpunkan dengan diseret atas muka-muka mereka) mereka digiring seraya diseret (ke neraka Jahanam, mereka itulah orang-orang yang paling buruk tempatnya) yaitu neraka Jahanam (dan paling sesat jalannya) paling keliru jalannya bila dibandingkan dengan selain mereka, sebab kekafirannya

ar: وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَیۡنَا مُوسَى ٱلۡكِتَـٰبَ وَجَعَلۡنَا مَعَهُۥۤ أَخَاهُ هَـٰرُونَ وَزِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ وَجَعَلْنَا مَعَهُ أَخَاهُ هَارُونَ وَزِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture and appointed with him his brother Aaron as an assistant
t.asad: AND, INDEED, [long before Muhammad] We vouchsafed revelation unto Moses, and appointed his brother Aaron to help him to bear his burden
t.yusuf-ali: (Before this,) We sent Moses The Book, and appointed his brother Aaron with him as minister
t.pickthall: We verily gave Moses the Scripture and placed with him his brother Aaron as henchman
t.arberry: We gave Moses the Book, and appointed with him his brother Aaron as minister
t.hilali-khan: And indeed We gave Musa (Moses) the Scripture [the Taurat (Torah)], and placed his brother Harun (Aaron) with him as a helper
t.itani: We gave Moses the Scripture, and appointed his brother Aaron as his assistant.
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed We gave Musa the Scripture, and placed his brother Harun with him as a helper;
t.qarib: And We gave Moses the Book and appointed his brother Aaron to help him
t.wahiduddin: We gave Moses the Book, and appointed his brother Aaron as his supporter
t.shakir: And certainly We gave Musa the Book and We appointed with him his brother Haroun an aider
t.haleem: We gave Moses the Book and appointed his brother Aaron to help him
t.maariful: Surely We gave Mūsā the Book, and appointed his brother Hārūn as a minister with him
t.khattab: We certainly gave Moses the Book and appointed his brother Aaron as his helper
t.ghali: And indeed We already brought Musa (Moses) the Book, and We made with him his brother Harun (Aaron) as minister
t.irving: We gave Moses the Book, and placed Aaron alongside him as a helpmate
t.iwq: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture and appointed with him his brother Aaron as an assistant

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya kami telah memberikan Al Kitab (Taurat) kepada Musa dan Kami telah menjadikan Harun saudaranya, menyertai dia sebagai wazir (pembantu).
t_id.sabiq: Nabi Muhammad saw. dihibur dengan peristiwa-peristiwa yang pernah dialami para rasul terdahulu. Sesungguhnya Kami telah menurunkan Tawrât kepada Mûsâ. Kami tugasi ia untuk menyampaikan risalah Kami. Dan Kami perkuat ia dengan menjadikan Hârûn, saudaranya, sebagai pembantu yang akan menolongnya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah memberikan kepada Musa kitab) Taurat (dan Kami telah menjadikan Harun saudaranya, menyertai dia sebagai wazir) yang membantunya di dalam menyampaikan tugas risalahnya.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya kami telah memberikan Al Kitab (Taurat) kepada Musa dan Kami telah menjadikan Harun saudaranya, menyertai dia sebagai wazir (pembantu).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد آتينا موسى الكتاب» التوراة «وجعلنا معه أخاه هارون وزيرا» معينا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَا مُوسَى ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ وَجَعَلۡنَا مَعَهُۥٓ أَخَاهُ هَٰرُونَ وَزِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah memberikan kepada Musa kitab) Taurat (dan Kami telah menjadikan Harun saudaranya, menyertai dia sebagai wazir) yang membantunya di dalam menyampaikan tugas risalahnya

ar: فَقُلۡنَا ٱذۡهَبَاۤ إِلَى ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلَّذِینَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِءَایَـٰتِنَا فَدَمَّرۡنَـٰهُمۡ تَدۡمِیرࣰا
ar_simple: فَقُلْنَا اذْهَبَا إِلَى الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا فَدَمَّرْنَاهُمْ تَدْمِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And We said, "Go both of you to the people who have denied Our signs." Then We destroyed them with [complete] destruction
t.asad: and We said, “Go you both unto the people who have given the lie to Our messages!” - and thereupon We broke those [sinners] to smithereens
t.yusuf-ali: And We command: "Go ye both, to the people who have rejected our Signs:" And those (people) We destroyed with utter destruction
t.pickthall: Then We said: Go together unto the folk who have denied Our revelations. Then We destroyed them, a complete destruction
t.arberry: and We said, 'Go to the people who have cried lies to Our signs'; then We destroyed them utterly
t.hilali-khan: And We said: "Go you both to the people who have denied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.)." Then We destroyed them with utter destruction
t.itani: We said, “Go to the people who rejected Our signs,” and We destroyed them completely.
t.mubarakpuri: And We said: "Go you both to the people who have denied Our Ayat." Then We destroyed them with utter destruction.
t.qarib: We said, "Go together to the people who have rejected Our signs." Then We destroyed them completely
t.wahiduddin: Then We said, "Go together to the people who have denied Our signs." We utterly destroyed them
t.shakir: Then We said: Go you both to the people who rejected Our communications; so We destroyed them with utter destruction
t.haleem: We said, ‘Go, both of you, to the people who have rejected Our signs.’ Later We destroyed those people utterly
t.maariful: So we said, “Go, both of you, to the people who have rejected Our signs” Then We annihilated them totally
t.khattab: We had ordered ˹them˺, “Go to the people who would deny Our signs.” Then We annihilated the deniers entirely
t.ghali: So We said, "Go you (both) to the people who have cried lies to Our signs." Then We destroyed them an utter destruction
t.irving: We said: "Go off to a folk who have denied Our signs." We utterly destroyed them
t.iwq: And We said, "Go both of you to the people who have denied Our signs." Then We destroyed them with complete destruction

t_id.kemenag: Kemudian Kami berfirman kepada keduanya: "Pergilah kamu berdua kepada kaum yang mendustakan ayat-ayat Kami". Maka Kami membinasakan mereka sehancur-hancurnya.
t_id.sabiq: Kemudian Kami berkata, "Pergilah kamu dan saudaramu kepada Fir'aun dan kaumnya." Kami perkuat juga ia dengan mukjizat yang menunjukkan kebenaran. Tetapi mereka tidak percaya pada itu semua dan justru mendustakan Mûsâ. Sebagai akibatnya, Kami membinasakan mereka sehancur-hancurnya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian Kami berfirman kepada keduanya, "Pergilah kamu berdua kepada kaum yang mendustakan ayat-ayat Kami") yakni bangsa Koptik, Firaun dan kaumnya, lalu keduanya berangkat ke negeri mereka, akan tetapi mereka mendustakannya. (Maka Kami membinasakan mereka sehancur-hancurnya) Kami menumpas mereka sampai binasa.
t_id.iwq: Kemudian Kami berfirman kepada keduanya: "Pergilah kamu berdua kepada kaum yang mendustakan ayat-ayat Kami". Maka Kami membinasakan mereka sehancur-hancurnya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فقلنا اذهبا إلى القوم الذين كذبوا بآياتنا» أي القبط فرعون وقومه فذهبا إليهم بالرسالة فكذبوهما «فدمرناهم تدميرا» أهلكناهم إهلاكا
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَقُلۡنَا ٱذۡهَبَآ إِلَى ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا فَدَمَّرۡنَٰهُمۡ تَدۡمِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian Kami berfirman kepada keduanya, "Pergilah kamu berdua kepada kaum yang mendustakan ayat-ayat Kami") yakni bangsa Koptik, Firaun dan kaumnya, lalu keduanya berangkat ke negeri mereka, akan tetapi mereka mendustakannya. (Maka Kami membinasakan mereka sehancur-hancurnya) Kami menumpas mereka sampai binasa

ar: وَقَوۡمَ نُوحࣲ لَّمَّا كَذَّبُوا۟ ٱلرُّسُلَ أَغۡرَقۡنَـٰهُمۡ وَجَعَلۡنَـٰهُمۡ لِلنَّاسِ ءَایَةࣰۖ وَأَعۡتَدۡنَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِینَ عَذَابًا أَلِیمࣰا
ar_simple: وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ لَمَّا كَذَّبُوا الرُّسُلَ أَغْرَقْنَاهُمْ وَجَعَلْنَاهُمْ لِلنَّاسِ آيَةً ۖ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا

t.sahih-int: And the people of Noah - when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them for mankind a sign. And We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment
t.asad: And [think of] the people of Noah: when they gave the lie to [one of] the apostles, We caused them to drown, and made them a symbol for all mankind: for, grievous suffering have We readied for all who [knowingly] do wrong
t.yusuf-ali: And the people of Noah,- when they rejected the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them as a Sign for mankind; and We have prepared for (all) wrong-doers a grievous Penalty
t.pickthall: And Noah's folk, when they denied the messengers, We drowned them and made of them a portent for mankind. We have prepared a painful doom for evil-doers
t.arberry: And the people of Noah, when they cried lies to the Messengers, We drowned them, and made them to be a sign to mankind; and We have prepared for the evildoers a painful chastisement
t.hilali-khan: And Nuh's (Noah) people, when they denied the Messengers We drowned them, and We made them as a sign for mankind. And We have prepared a painful torment for the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc)
t.itani: And the people of Noah: when they rejected the messengers, We drowned them, and made them a lesson for mankind. We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful retribution.
t.mubarakpuri: And Nuh's people, when they denied the Messengers, We drowned them, and We made them as a sign for mankind. And We have prepared a painful torment for the wrongdoers.
t.qarib: And the people of Noah-when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them a sign for people to come. And We have prepared a painful punishment for the unjust
t.wahiduddin: We drowned the people of Noah also when they rejected their messengers and We made them an example to all mankind. We have prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers
t.shakir: And the people of Nuh, when they rejected the apostles, We drowned them, and made them a sign for men, and We have prepared a painful punishment for the unjust
t.haleem: The people of Noah, too: when they rejected their messengers, We drowned them and made them an example to all people. We have prepared a painful torment for the evildoers
t.maariful: As for the people of NūH, when they rejected the messengers, We drowned them and made them an example for mankind, and We have prepared a painful punishment for the transgressors
t.khattab: And when the people of Noah rejected the messengers, We drowned them, making them an example to humanity. And We have prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers
t.ghali: And the people of Nuh, (Noah) as soon as they cried lies to the Messengers, We drowned them, and We made them to be a sign to mankind; and We have readied for the unjust a painful torment
t.irving: So when Noah´s folk rejected the messengers, We let them drown and set them up as a sign for mankind. We reserve painful torment for wrongdoers
t.iwq: And the people of Noah - when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them for mankind a sign. And We have prepared for those who do wrong a painful punishment

t_id.kemenag: Dan (telah Kami binasakan) kaum Nuh tatkala mereka mendustakan rasul-rasul. Kami tenggelamkan mereka dan kami jadikan (cerita) mereka itu pelajaran bagi manusia. Dan Kami telah menyediakan bagi orang-orang zalim azab yang pedih;
t_id.sabiq: Demikian pula yang Kami lakukan sebelum Mûsâ terhadap kaum Nûh yang mendustakan rasulnya. Barangsiapa mendustakan seorang rasul, maka sebenarnya dia telah mendustakan semua para rasul. Sesungguhnya Kami telah menenggelamkan mereka dengan badai topan yang dapat dijadikan pelajaran oleh semua manusia. Kami telah menjadikan untuk mereka dan setiap orang musyrik siksa yang pedih di akhirat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan) ingatlah (kaum Nuh tatkala mereka mendustakan Rasul-rasul) mereka mendustakan Nabi Nuh, mengingat Nabi Nuh tinggal bersama mereka dalam kurun waktu yang lama sekali, maka diungkapkan dalam ayat ini seolah-olah Nabi Nuh menduduki tempat Rasul-rasul. Atau karena dengan mendustakan Nabi Nuh maka seolah-olah mereka mendustakan Rasul-rasul lainnya yang membawa risalah yang sama dengan apa yang dibawa oleh Nabi Nuh, yaitu ajaran Tauhid (Kami tenggelamkan mereka) lafal ayat ini menjadi jawab dari Lamma (dan Kami jadikan cerita mereka bagi manusia) sesudah mereka (sebagai tanda) pelajaran (dan Kami telah menyediakan) di akhirat (untuk orang-orang yang zalim) yakni orang-orang kafir (azab yang pedih) siksaan yang sangat menyakitkan di samping azab yang telah mereka rasakan sewaktu hidup di dunia.
t_id.iwq: Dan (telah Kami binasakan) kaum Nuh tatkala mereka mendustakan rasul-rasul. Kami tenggelamkan mereka dan kami jadikan (cerita) mereka itu pelajaran bagi manusia. Dan Kami telah menyediakan bagi orang-orang zalim azab yang pedih;

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «و» اذكر «قوم نوح لما كذبوا الرسل» بتكذيبهم نوحا لطول لبثه فيهم فكأنه رسل، أو لأن تكذيبه تكذيب لباقي الرسل لاشتراكهم في المجيء بالتوحيد «أغرقناهم» جواب لما «وجعلناهم للناس» بعدهم «آية» عبرة «وأعتدنا» في الآخرة «للظالمين» الكافرين «عذابا أليما» مؤلما سوى ما يحل بهم في الدنيا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَوۡمَ نُوحٖ لَّمَّا كَذَّبُواْ ٱلرُّسُلَ أَغۡرَقۡنَٰهُمۡ وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ لِلنَّاسِ ءَايَةٗۖ وَأَعۡتَدۡنَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ عَذَابًا أَلِيمٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan) ingatlah (kaum Nuh tatkala mereka mendustakan Rasul-rasul) mereka mendustakan Nabi Nuh, mengingat Nabi Nuh tinggal bersama mereka dalam kurun waktu yang lama sekali, maka diungkapkan dalam ayat ini seolah-olah Nabi Nuh menduduki tempat Rasul-rasul. Atau karena dengan mendustakan Nabi Nuh maka seolah-olah mereka mendustakan Rasul-rasul lainnya yang membawa risalah yang sama dengan apa yang dibawa oleh Nabi Nuh, yaitu ajaran Tauhid (Kami tenggelamkan mereka) lafal ayat ini menjadi jawab dari Lamma (dan Kami jadikan cerita mereka bagi manusia) sesudah mereka (sebagai tanda) pelajaran (dan Kami telah menyediakan) di akhirat (untuk orang-orang yang zalim) yakni orang-orang kafir (azab yang pedih) siksaan yang sangat menyakitkan di samping azab yang telah mereka rasakan sewaktu hidup di dunia

ar: وَعَادࣰا وَثَمُودَا۟ وَأَصۡحَـٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونَۢا بَیۡنَ ذَ ٰلِكَ كَثِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وَعَادًا وَثَمُودَ وَأَصْحَابَ الرَّسِّ وَقُرُونًا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And [We destroyed] 'Aad and Thamud and the companions of the well and many generations between them
t.asad: And [remember how We punished the tribes of] Ad and Thamud and the people of Ar-Rass, and many generations [of sinners] in-between
t.yusuf-ali: As also 'Ad and Thamud, and the Companions of the Rass, and many a generation between them
t.pickthall: And (the tribes of) A'ad and Thamud, and the dwellers in Ar-Rass, and many generations in between
t.arberry: And Ad, and Thamood, and the men of Er-Rass, and between that generations a many
t.hilali-khan: And (also) 'Ad and Thamud, and the dwellers of Ar-Rass, and many generations in between
t.itani: And Aad, and Thamood, and the inhabitants of Arras, and many generations in between.
t.mubarakpuri: And (also) `Ad and Thamud, and the Dwellers of Ar-Rass, and many generations in between.
t.qarib: [And the people of] Aad and Thamud, the people of the Al Rass and many generations in between
t.wahiduddin: to 'Ad, and Thamud, and the people of al-Rass, and as We did for the many a generation between them
t.shakir: And Ad and Samood and the dwellers of the Rass and many generations between them
t.haleem: as We did for the people of Ad, Thamud, and al-Rass, and many generations in between
t.maariful: And (We annihilated also the peoples of) ‘Ād and Thamūd and the people of Rass, and many generations in between them
t.khattab: Also ˹We destroyed˺ ’Ȃd, Thamûd, and the people of the Water-pit, as well as many peoples in between
t.ghali: And Aad, and Thamud, and the companions (i.e., inhabitants) of Ar-Rass, and between them (Literally: that) many generations
t.irving: such as Ad and Thamud and the companions at the Well and many generations in between
t.iwq: And We destroyed 'Aad and Thamud and the companions of the well and many generations between them

t_id.kemenag: dan (Kami binasakan) kaum 'Aad dan Tsamud dan penduduk Rass dan banyak (lagi) generasi-generasi di antara kaum-kaum tersebut.
t_id.sabiq: Demikian pula Kami telah menghancurkan 'Ad, Tsamûd dan penduduk al-Rass(1) tatkala mereka mendustakan rasul-rasul mereka. Kami juga telah menghancurkan banyak kaum yang hidup di antara masa kaum Nûh dan 'Ad. Mereka mendapat balasan sebagaimana orang-orang yang zalim. (1) Al-Rass, seperti disebutkan dalam Al-Mufradât karya al-Râghib al-Ashfahânî, berarti 'lembah'. Al-Ashfahânî berdalil dengan sepnggal bait syair: wa hunna al-wâdî al-rass ka al-yad li al-fam. Penduduk al-Rass yang disebutkan pada ayat di atas adalah kaum yang selalu menyembah patung. Kemudian Allah mengutus kepada mereka Nabi Syu'aib a. s. Di dalam al-Qur'ân, kaum Syu'aib terkadang disebut sebagai penduduk Aykah, yang berarti tempat yang dipenuhi pepohonan yang rindang. Terkadang disebut juga dengan penduduk al-Rass, yaitu sebuah lembah yang banyak mengandung kebaikan. Hal itu menunjukkan banyaknya nikmat Allah yang diberikan kepada mereka. Tapi mereka mengingkarinya dan menyembah patung.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan) ingatlah (kaum Ad) yakni kaum Nabi Hud (dan Tsamud) kaum Nabi Saleh (dan penduduk Rass) nama sebuah sumur; Nabi mereka menurut suatu pendapat adalah Nabi Syuaib, tetapi menurut pendapat yang lain bukan Nabi Syuaib. Mereka tinggal di sekitar sumur itu, kemudian sumur itu amblas berikut orang-orang yang tinggal di sekitarnya dan rumah-rumah mereka pun ikut amblas (dan banyak lagi generasi-generasi) kaum-kaum (di antara kaum-kaum tersebut) yakni antara kaum Ad dan penduduk Rass.
t_id.iwq: dan (Kami binasakan) kaum 'Aad dan Tsamud dan penduduk Rass dan banyak (lagi) generasi-generasi di antara kaum-kaum tersebut.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «و» اذكر «عادا» يوم هود «وثمود» قوم صالح «وأصحاب الرَّسَّ» اسم بئر، ونبيهم قيل شعيب وقيل غيره كانوا قعودا حولها فانهارت بهم وبمنازلهم «وقرونا» أقواما «بين ذلك كثيرا» أي بين عاد وأصحاب الرَّسَّ
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَعَادٗا وَثَمُودَاْ وَأَصۡحَٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونَۢا بَيۡنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرٗا (وَأَصْحَابَ الرَّسِّ: أَصْحَابَ البِئْرِ, وَقُرُونًا: أُمَمًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan) ingatlah (kaum Ad) yakni kaum Nabi Hud (dan Tsamud) kaum Nabi Saleh (dan penduduk Rass) nama sebuah sumur; Nabi mereka menurut suatu pendapat adalah Nabi Syuaib, tetapi menurut pendapat yang lain bukan Nabi Syuaib. Mereka tinggal di sekitar sumur itu, kemudian sumur itu amblas berikut orang-orang yang tinggal di sekitarnya dan rumah-rumah mereka pun ikut amblas (dan banyak lagi generasi-generasi) kaum-kaum (di antara kaum-kaum tersebut) yakni antara kaum Ad dan penduduk Rass

ar: وَكُلࣰّا ضَرَبۡنَا لَهُ ٱلۡأَمۡثَـٰلَۖ وَكُلࣰّا تَبَّرۡنَا تَتۡبِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وَكُلًّا ضَرَبْنَا لَهُ الْأَمْثَالَ ۖ وَكُلًّا تَبَّرْنَا تَتْبِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And for each We presented examples [as warnings], and each We destroyed with [total] destruction
t.asad: and unto each of them did We proffer lessons and each of them did We destroy with utter destruction
t.yusuf-ali: To each one We set forth Parables and examples; and each one We broke to utter annihilation (for their sins)
t.pickthall: Each (of them) We warned by examples, and each (of them) We brought to utter ruin
t.arberry: for each We struck similitudes, and each We ruined utterly
t.hilali-khan: And for each of them We put forward examples (as proofs and lessons, etc.), and each (of them) We brought to utter ruin (because of their disbelief and evil deeds)
t.itani: To each We presented the parables; and each We devastated utterly.
t.mubarakpuri: And for each We put forward examples, and each We brought to utter ruin.
t.qarib: in each case, We, [warn them with] examples, and each We utterly destroyed
t.wahiduddin: To each of them We gave warnings and each of them We destroyed completely
t.shakir: And to every one We gave examples and every one did We destroy with utter destruction
t.haleem: To each of them We gave warnings, and each of them We destroyed completely
t.maariful: To each (of them) We cited examples, and each of them We brought to utter ruin
t.khattab: For each We set forth ˹various˺ lessons, and We ultimately destroyed each
t.ghali: And for each We struck similitudes, and each We annihilated utterly (Literally: annihilated annihilation)
t.irving: We have made up parables for each, and each We followed up with doom
t.iwq: And for each We presented examples as warnings, and each We destroyed with total destruction

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami jadikan bagi masing-masing mereka perumpamaan dan masing-masing mereka itu benar benar telah Kami binasakan dengan sehancur-hancurnya.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya Kami telah memperingatkan semua kaum tersebut. Kami telah sebutkan kepada mereka beberapa nasihat dan tamsil ibarat yang bermanfaat. Tetapi mereka tidak memetik pelajaran dari situ. Maka Kami timpakan mereka semua dengan azab, dan Kami hancurkan negeri mereka dengan sehancur- hancurnya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami jadikan bagi masing-masing mereka tamsil) untuk menegakkan hujah terhadap mereka, oleh karenanya tidak sekali-kali Kami membinasakan mereka melainkan sesudah mereka Kami beri peringatan (dan masing-masing mereka itu benar-benar telah Kami binasakan dengan sehancur-hancurnya) yakni Kami tumpas mereka hingga binasa, disebabkan mereka telah mendustakan nabi-nabi mereka.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami jadikan bagi masing-masing mereka perumpamaan dan masing-masing mereka itu benar benar telah Kami binasakan dengan sehancur-hancurnya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وكلا ضربنا له الأمثال» في إقامة الحجة عليهم فلم نهلكهم إلا بعد الإنذار «وكلاٌ تبَّرنا تتبيرا» أهلكنا إهلاكا بتكذيبهم أنبياءهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَكُلّٗا ضَرَبۡنَا لَهُ ٱلۡأَمۡثَٰلَۖ وَكُلّٗا تَبَّرۡنَا تَتۡبِيرٗا (الْأَمْثَالَ: الحُجَجَ, تَبَّرْنَا: أَهْلَكْنَا وَدَمَّرْنَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami jadikan bagi masing-masing mereka tamsil) untuk menegakkan hujah terhadap mereka, oleh karenanya tidak sekali-kali Kami membinasakan mereka melainkan sesudah mereka Kami beri peringatan (dan masing-masing mereka itu benar-benar telah Kami binasakan dengan sehancur-hancurnya) yakni Kami tumpas mereka hingga binasa, disebabkan mereka telah mendustakan nabi-nabi mereka

ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَتَوۡا۟ عَلَى ٱلۡقَرۡیَةِ ٱلَّتِیۤ أُمۡطِرَتۡ مَطَرَ ٱلسَّوۡءِۚ أَفَلَمۡ یَكُونُوا۟ یَرَوۡنَهَاۚ بَلۡ كَانُوا۟ لَا یَرۡجُونَ نُشُورࣰا
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ أَتَوْا عَلَى الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي أُمْطِرَتْ مَطَرَ السَّوْءِ ۚ أَفَلَمْ يَكُونُوا يَرَوْنَهَا ۚ بَلْ كَانُوا لَا يَرْجُونَ نُشُورًا

t.sahih-int: And they have already come upon the town which was showered with a rain of evil. So have they not seen it? But they are not expecting resurrection
t.asad: And they [who now deny Our messages] must surely have come across that town which was rained upon by a rain of evil: have they, then, never beheld it [with their minds eye]? But nay, they would not believe in resurrection
t.yusuf-ali: And the (Unbelievers) must indeed have passed by the town on which was rained a shower of evil: did they not then see it (with their own eyes)? But they fear not the Resurrection
t.pickthall: And indeed they have passed by the township whereon was rained the fatal rain. Can it be that they have not seen it? Nay, but they hope for no resurrection
t.arberry: Surely they have come by the city that was rained on by an evil rain; what, have they not seen it? Nay, but they look for no upraising
t.hilali-khan: And indeed they have passed by the town [of Prophet Lout (Lot)] on which was rained the evil rain. Did they (disbelievers) not then see it (with their own eyes)? Nay! But they used not to expect for any resurrection
t.itani: And they came upon the city that was drenched by the terrible rain. Did they not see it? But they do not expect resurrection.
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed they have passed by the town on which was rained the evil rain. Did they not then see it Nay! But they used not to expect any resurrection.
t.qarib: They must surely have come upon the town that was showered with a horrible rain; have they not seen it? But they do not expect resurrection
t.wahiduddin: Indeed they must have come upon the town on which an evil rain had poured down. Did they not see it? Yet they have no faith in the Resurrection
t.shakir: And certainly they have (often) passed by the town on which was rained an evil rain; did they not then see it? Nay! they did not hope to be raised again
t.haleem: These disbelievers must have passed by the town that was destroyed by the terrible rain––did they not see it? Yet they do not expect to be raised from the dead
t.maariful: Indeed they (the pagans of Makkah) have passed by the town that was afflicted by an evil rain sent down to it. So, have they not been seeing it? Rather, they do not believe in Resurrection
t.khattab: They have certainly passed by the city ˹of Sodom˺, which had been showered with a dreadful rain ˹of stones˺. Have they not seen its ruins? But they do not expect to be resurrected
t.ghali: And indeed they already came up to the town which was rained on by a woeful rain. Have they then not seen it? No indeed, (but) they did not hope for an uprising (i.e., resurrection)
t.irving: They have come to the town on which an evil rain poured down. Had they not seen it? Indeed they had not expected to be reborn
t.iwq: And they have already come upon the town which was showered with a rain of evil. Thus have they not seen it? But they are not expecting resurrection

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya mereka (kaum musyrik Mekah) telah melalui sebuah negeri (Sadum) yang (dulu) dihujani dengan hujan yang sejelek-jeleknya (hujan batu). Maka apakah mereka tidak menyaksikan runtuhan itu; bahkan adalah mereka itu tidak mengharapkan akan kebangkitan.
t_id.sabiq: Dalam perjalanan mereka ke Syam, orang-orang Quraisy selalu melewati negeri kaum Lûth yang dihujani dengan hujan yang paling jelek karena membawa bebatuan dari tanah yang terbakar. Apakah mereka tidak memperhatikan negeri itu dan mengambil pelajaran dari musibah yang dialami penduduknya? Mereka tentu telah melihat semua itu, tetapi bukan untuk mengambil pelajaran. Sebab, mereka tidak percaya kepada hari kiamat dan kebangkitan. Mereka pun tidak memperkirakan hari di saat mereka akan dibangkitkan untuk dimintakan pertanggungjawaban.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah melalui) yakni orang-orang kafir Mekah itu sering melewati (sebuah negeri yang dihujani dengan hujan yang sejelek-jeleknya) lafal As Sau' adalah bentuk Mashdar dari lafal Saa-a. Maksudnya mereka telah dihujani dengan batu-batu; negeri tempat tinggal mereka adalah suatu kota yang paling besar bagi kaum Nabi Luth, kemudian Allah membinasakan mereka sebab mereka gemar melakukan perbuatan yang keji. (Maka apakah mereka tidak menyaksikan bekas-bekasnya) di dalam perjalanan mereka menuju ke negeri Syam, kemudian mereka mengambil pelajaran darinya? Istifham atau kata tanya pada ayat ini mengandung makna Taqrir, maksudnya, hendaklah mereka mengambil pelajaran dari bekas-bekas itu (bahkan adalah mereka itu tidak mengharapkan) mereka tidak takut sama sekali (akan kebangkitan) maksudnya hari mereka dihidupkan kembali, mereka sama sekali tidak beriman kepadanya.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya mereka (kaum musyrik Mekah) telah melalui sebuah negeri (Sadum) yang (dulu) dihujani dengan hujan yang sejelek-jeleknya (hujan batu). Maka apakah mereka tidak menyaksikan runtuhan itu; bahkan adalah mereka itu tidak mengharapkan akan kebangkitan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد أتوا» أي مرَّ كفار مكة «على القرية التي أمطرت مطر السوء» مصدر ساء أي بالحجارة وهي عظمى قرى قوم لوط فأهلك الله أهلها لفعلهم الفاحشة «أفلم يكونوا يرونَها» في سفرهم إلى الشام فيعتبرون، والاستفهام للتقرير «بل كانوا لا يرجون» يخافون «نشورا» بعثا فلا يؤمنون
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَتَوۡاْ عَلَى ٱلۡقَرۡيَةِ ٱلَّتِيٓ أُمۡطِرَتۡ مَطَرَ ٱلسَّوۡءِۚ أَفَلَمۡ يَكُونُواْ يَرَوۡنَهَاۚ بَلۡ كَانُواْ لَا يَرۡجُونَ نُشُورٗا (مَطَرَ السَّوْءِ: حِجَارَةً مِنَ السَّمَاءِ أَهْلَكَتْهُمِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah melalui) yakni orang-orang kafir Mekah itu sering melewati (sebuah negeri yang dihujani dengan hujan yang sejelek-jeleknya) lafal As Sau' adalah bentuk Mashdar dari lafal Saa-a. Maksudnya mereka telah dihujani dengan batu-batu; negeri tempat tinggal mereka adalah suatu kota yang paling besar bagi kaum Nabi Luth, kemudian Allah membinasakan mereka sebab mereka gemar melakukan perbuatan yang keji. (Maka apakah mereka tidak menyaksikan bekas-bekasnya) di dalam perjalanan mereka menuju ke negeri Syam, kemudian mereka mengambil pelajaran darinya? Istifham atau kata tanya pada ayat ini mengandung makna Taqrir, maksudnya, hendaklah mereka mengambil pelajaran dari bekas-bekas itu (bahkan adalah mereka itu tidak mengharapkan) mereka tidak takut sama sekali (akan kebangkitan) maksudnya hari mereka dihidupkan kembali, mereka sama sekali tidak beriman kepadanya

ar: وَإِذَا رَأَوۡكَ إِن یَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِی بَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ رَسُولًا
ar_simple: وَإِذَا رَأَوْكَ إِنْ يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَٰذَا الَّذِي بَعَثَ اللَّهُ رَسُولًا

t.sahih-int: And when they see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger
t.asad: Hence, whenever they consider thee, [O Muhammad,] they but make thee a target of their mockery, [saying:] “Is this the one whom God has sent as an apostle
t.yusuf-ali: When they see thee, they treat thee no otherwise than in mockery: "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger
t.pickthall: And when they see thee (O Muhammad) they treat thee only as a jest (saying): Is this he whom Allah sendeth as a messenger
t.arberry: And when they see thee, they take thee in mockery only: 'What, is this he whom God sent forth as a Messenger
t.hilali-khan: And when they see you (O Muhammad SAW), they treat you only as a mockery (saying):"Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a Messenger
t.itani: And when they see you, they take you for nothing but mockery: “Is this the one God sent as a messenger?”
t.mubarakpuri: And when they see you, they treat you only in mockery (saying): "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a Messenger"
t.qarib: And whenever they see you [Prophet] they ridicule you, [saying], "Is this the one God sent as a messenger
t.wahiduddin: Whenever they see you they only make a mockery of you -- "Is this the one God has sent as His Messenger
t.shakir: And when they see you, they do not take you for aught but a mockery: Is this he whom Allah has raised to be an apostle
t.haleem: Whenever they see you [Prophet] they ridicule you: ‘Is this the one God has sent as a messenger
t.maariful: When they see you, they take you only as a laughing stock, (saying) “Is this the man whom Allah has sent as a messenger
t.khattab: When they see you ˹O Prophet˺, they only make fun of you, ˹saying,˺ “Is this the one that Allah has sent as a messenger
t.ghali: And when they see you, they decidedly take you to themselves for nothing except in mockery, "Is this he whom Allah has sent forth as a Messenger
t.irving: Whenever they see you, they merely treat you as a laughingstock: "Is this the person whom God has sent as a messenger
t.iwq: And when they see you, O Muhammad, they take you not except in ridicule, saying, "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger

t_id.kemenag: Dan apabila mereka melihat kamu (Muhammad), mereka hanyalah menjadikan kamu sebagai ejekan (dengan mengatakan): "Inikah orangnya yang di utus Allah sebagai Rasul?.
t_id.sabiq: Jika mereka melihat kamu, mereka menjadikanmu sebagai bahan ejekan dan cacian. Sebagian mereka berkata kepada yang lainnya, "Inikah orang yang diutus oleh Allah kepada kami sebagai rasul yang harus kami ikuti?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan apabila mereka melihat kamu, maka tidak lain) (mereka menjadikan kamu sebagai ejekan) sasaran ejekan mereka, di dalam ejekannya itu mereka mengatakan, ("Inikah orangnya yang diutus oleh Allah sebagai Rasul?) menurut pengakuannya. Mereka mengatakan demikian dengan nada menghina dan menganggap remeh risalah yang dibawanya.
t_id.iwq: Dan apabila mereka melihat kamu (Muhammad), mereka hanyalah menjadikan kamu sebagai ejekan (dengan mengatakan): "Inikah orangnya yang di utus Allah sebagai Rasul?.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإذا رأوْك إن» ما «يتخذونك إلا هزؤا» مهزوءا به يقولن «أهذا الذي بعث الله رسولا» في دعواه محتقرين له عن الرسالة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِذَا رَأَوۡكَ إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي بَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ رَسُولًا (كَادَ لَيُضِلُّنَا: قَارَبَ أَنْ يَصْرِفَنَا عَنْ عِبَادَةِ أَصْنَامِنَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan apabila mereka melihat kamu, maka tidak lain) (mereka menjadikan kamu sebagai ejekan) sasaran ejekan mereka, di dalam ejekannya itu mereka mengatakan, ("Inikah orangnya yang diutus oleh Allah sebagai Rasul?) menurut pengakuannya. Mereka mengatakan demikian dengan nada menghina dan menganggap remeh risalah yang dibawanya

ar: إِن كَادَ لَیُضِلُّنَا عَنۡ ءَالِهَتِنَا لَوۡلَاۤ أَن صَبَرۡنَا عَلَیۡهَاۚ وَسَوۡفَ یَعۡلَمُونَ حِینَ یَرَوۡنَ ٱلۡعَذَابَ مَنۡ أَضَلُّ سَبِیلًا
ar_simple: إِنْ كَادَ لَيُضِلُّنَا عَنْ آلِهَتِنَا لَوْلَا أَنْ صَبَرْنَا عَلَيْهَا ۚ وَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ حِينَ يَرَوْنَ الْعَذَابَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا

t.sahih-int: He almost would have misled us from our gods had we not been steadfast in [worship of] them." But they are going to know, when they see the punishment, who is farthest astray in [his] way
t.asad: Indeed, he would well-nigh have led us astray from our deities, had we not been [so] steadfastly attached to them!” But in time, when they see the suffering [that awaits them], they will come to know who it was that went farthest astray from the path [of truth]
t.yusuf-ali: He indeed would well-nigh have misled us from our gods, had it not been that we were constant to them!" - Soon will they know, when they see the Penalty, who it is that is most misled in Path
t.pickthall: He would have led us far away from our gods if we had not been staunch to them. They will know, when they behold the doom, who is more astray as to the road
t.arberry: Wellnigh he had led us astray from our gods, but that we kept steadfast to them.' Assuredly they shall know, when they see the chastisement, who is further astray from the way
t.hilali-khan: He would have nearly misled us from our aliha (gods), had it not been that we were patient and constant in their worship!" And they will know when they see the torment, who it is that is most astray from the (Right) Path
t.itani: “He nearly led us away from our gods, had we not patiently adhered to them.” But they will know, when they witness the torment, who is further away from the way.
t.mubarakpuri: "He would have nearly misled us from our gods, had it not been that we were patient and constant in their worship!" And they will know, when they see the torment, who it is that is most astray from the path!
t.qarib: He would almost have misled us from our gods had we not stood firmly by them." But soon, when they see the punishment, they will know who is the most misguided
t.wahiduddin: Indeed, he would well-nigh have led us astray from our deities, had we not been [so] steadfastly attached to them!" When they behold the punishment, they shall realize who strayed furthest from the right path
t.shakir: He had well-nigh led us astray from our gods had we not adhered to them patiently! And they will know, when they see the punishment, who is straying farther off from the path
t.haleem: He might almost have led us astray from our gods if we had not stood so firmly by them.’ When they see the punishment, they will know who is furthest from the path
t.maariful: He would have almost led us astray from our gods, had we not been so firm towards them.” They will know when they will see the punishment, who is farther astray in his way
t.khattab: He would have almost tricked us away from our gods, had we not been so devoted to them.” ˹But˺ soon they will know, when they face the punishment, who is far astray from the ˹Right˺ Way
t.ghali: Decidedly he had almost made us, indeed, to err away from our gods, had we not (suffered ourselves) to endure patiently with them." And eventually they will know, while they see the tornment, who has erred further from the way
t.irving: He almost led us away from our gods, if we had not acted so patient towards them." They will know who is furthest off the track when they see the torment
t.iwq: He almost would have misled us from our gods had we not been steadfast in worship of them." But they are going to know, when they see the punishment, who is farthest astray in his way

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya hampirlah ia menyesatkan kita dari sembahan-sembahan kita, seandainya kita tidak sabar(menyembah)nya" dan mereka kelak akan mengetahui di saat mereka melihat azab, siapa yang paling sesat jalannya.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya lelaki itu telah diberikan retorika yang baik dan argumentasi yang kuat sehingga dapat menarik perhatian para pendengar. Dia telah merusak akidah kita sampai-sampai hampir menjauhkan kita dari sembahan-sembahan kita dan mengalihkan kita kepada Tuhannya. Akan tetapi kita tetap teguh pada tuhan-tuhan dan agama kita." Kelak akan Kami jelaskan perkara yang sebenarnya ketika mereka melihat siksa pada hari kiamat. Saat itu mereka akan tahu siapa yang paling sesat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya) lafal In adalah bentuk Takhfif dari lafal Inna, sedangkan isimnya tidak disebutkan, jadi bentuk lengkapnya adalah Innahuu, artinya, sesungguhnya ia (hampirlah ia menyesatkan kita) memalingkan diri kita (dari sesembahan-sesembahan kita, seandainya kita tidak sabar menyembahnya.") niscaya kita dapat dipalingkan dari sesembahan-sesembahan kita olehnya. Maka Allah berfirman, ("Dan mereka kelak akan mengetahui di saat mereka melihat azab) secara terang-terangan kelak di akhirat (siapa yang paling sesat jalannya") yang paling keliru tuntunannya, apakah mereka ataukah orang-orang yang beriman?
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya hampirlah ia menyesatkan kita dari sembahan-sembahan kita, seandainya kita tidak sabar(menyembah)nya" dan mereka kelak akan mengetahui di saat mereka melihat azab, siapa yang paling sesat jalannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن» مخففة من الثقيلة واسمها محذوف أي إنه «كاد ليضلنا» يصرفنا «عن آلهتنا لولا أن صبرنا عليها» لصرفنا عنها، قال تعالى: «وسوف يعلمون حين يرون العذاب» عينانا في الآخرة «من أضلُّ سبيلا» أخطأ طريقا، أهم أم المؤمنون
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِن كَادَ لَيُضِلُّنَا عَنۡ ءَالِهَتِنَا لَوۡلَآ أَن صَبَرۡنَا عَلَيۡهَاۚ وَسَوۡفَ يَعۡلَمُونَ حِينَ يَرَوۡنَ ٱلۡعَذَابَ مَنۡ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya) lafal In adalah bentuk Takhfif dari lafal Inna, sedangkan isimnya tidak disebutkan, jadi bentuk lengkapnya adalah Innahuu, artinya, sesungguhnya ia (hampirlah ia menyesatkan kita) memalingkan diri kita (dari sesembahan-sesembahan kita, seandainya kita tidak sabar menyembahnya.") niscaya kita dapat dipalingkan dari sesembahan-sesembahan kita olehnya. Maka Allah berfirman, ("Dan mereka kelak akan mengetahui di saat mereka melihat azab) secara terang-terangan kelak di akhirat (siapa yang paling sesat jalannya") yang paling keliru tuntunannya, apakah mereka ataukah orang-orang yang beriman

ar: أَرَءَیۡتَ مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَـٰهَهُۥ هَوَىٰهُ أَفَأَنتَ تَكُونُ عَلَیۡهِ وَكِیلًا
ar_simple: أَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُ هَوَاهُ أَفَأَنْتَ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ وَكِيلًا

t.sahih-int: Have you seen the one who takes as his god his own desire? Then would you be responsible for him
