t.asad: Hast thou ever considered [the kind of man] who makes his own desires his deity? Couldst thou, then, [O Prophet,] be held responsible for him
t.yusuf-ali: Seest thou such a one as taketh for his god his own passion (or impulse)? Couldst thou be a disposer of affairs for him
t.pickthall: Hast thou seen him who chooseth for his god his own lust? Wouldst thou then be guardian over him
t.arberry: Hast thou seen him who has taken his caprice to be his god? Wilt thou be a guardian over them
t.hilali-khan: Have you (O Muhammad SAW) seen him who has taken as his ilah (god) his own desire? Would you then be a Wakil (a disposer of his affairs or a watcher) over him
t.itani: Have you seen him who chose his desire as his god? Would you be an agent for him?
t.mubarakpuri: Have you seen him who has taken as his god his own vain desire Would you then be a guardian over him
t.qarib: Have you seen the one who has taken his own whims as his god? Are you responsible for him
t.wahiduddin: Have you seen him who has taken his own desire to be his god? Can you be a guardian over him
t.shakir: Have you seen him who takes his low desires for his god? Will you then be a protector over him
t.haleem: Think [Prophet] of the man who has taken his own passion as a god: are you to be his guardian
t.maariful: Tell me about the one who has taken his desire as his god, would you then, become a guardian for him
t.khattab: Have you seen ˹O Prophet˺ the one who has taken their own desires as their god? Will you then be a keeper over them
t.ghali: Have you seen him who has taken to himself his prejudice to be his god? Will you then be a constant trustee over him
t.irving: Have you seen someone who has taken his own passion as his god? Would you act as a trustee for him
t.iwq: Have you seen the one who takes as his god his own desire? Then would you be responsible for him

t_id.kemenag: Terangkanlah kepadaku tentang orang yang menjadikan hawa nafsunya sebagai tuhannya. Maka apakah kamu dapat menjadi pemelihara atasnya?,
t_id.sabiq: Apakah kamu melihat, wahai Rasul, kesesatan orang yang mengikuti hawa nafsunya sehingga dia menyembah bebatuan yang tidak dapat mendatangkan bahaya dan manfaat? Akan tetapi kamu diutus hanya sebagai pemberi peringatan dan kabar gembira, bukan untuk memaksa mereka untuk beriman dan mendapat hidayah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Terangkanlah kepadaku) ceritakanlah kepada-Ku (tentang orang yang menjadikan hawa nafsunya sebagai tuhannya) maksudnya orang-orang yang menurutkan hawa nafsunya; dalam ungkapan ayat ini Maf'ul kedua didahulukan mengingat kedudukannya yang penting, yaitu lafal Ilaahahu. Sedangkan jumlah Manittakhadza Hawaahu adalah Maf'ul Awwal dari lafal Ara-aita, dan Maf'ul yang kedua adalah lafal Ilaahahu yang didahulukan tadi. (Maka apakah kamu dapat menjadi pemeliharanya?) yang dapat memelihara dia untuk tidak mengikuti hawa nafsunya? Tentu saja tidak.
t_id.iwq: Terangkanlah kepadaku tentang orang yang menjadikan hawa nafsunya sebagai tuhannya. Maka apakah kamu dapat menjadi pemelihara atasnya?,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أرأيت» أخبرني «من اتخذ إلههُ هواه» أي مُهويه قدَّم المفعول الثاني لأنه أهمّ وجملة من اتخذ مفعول أول لرأيت والثاني «أفأنت تكون عليه وكيلا» حافظا تحفظه عن ابتاع هواه لا
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَرَءَيۡتَ مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُۥ هَوَىٰهُ أَفَأَنتَ تَكُونُ عَلَيۡهِ وَكِيلًا (أَرَأَيْتَ: أَخْبِرْنِي)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Terangkanlah kepadaku) ceritakanlah kepada-Ku (tentang orang yang menjadikan hawa nafsunya sebagai tuhannya) maksudnya orang-orang yang menurutkan hawa nafsunya; dalam ungkapan ayat ini Maf'ul kedua didahulukan mengingat kedudukannya yang penting, yaitu lafal Ilaahahu. Sedangkan jumlah Manittakhadza Hawaahu adalah Maf'ul Awwal dari lafal Ara-aita, dan Maf'ul yang kedua adalah lafal Ilaahahu yang didahulukan tadi. (Maka apakah kamu dapat menjadi pemeliharanya?) yang dapat memelihara dia untuk tidak mengikuti hawa nafsunya? Tentu saja tidak

ar: أَمۡ تَحۡسَبُ أَنَّ أَكۡثَرَهُمۡ یَسۡمَعُونَ أَوۡ یَعۡقِلُونَۚ إِنۡ هُمۡ إِلَّا كَٱلۡأَنۡعَـٰمِ بَلۡ هُمۡ أَضَلُّ سَبِیلًا
ar_simple: أَمْ تَحْسَبُ أَنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ أَوْ يَعْقِلُونَ ۚ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا كَالْأَنْعَامِ ۖ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا

t.sahih-int: Or do you think that most of them hear or reason? They are not except like livestock. Rather, they are [even] more astray in [their] way
t.asad: Or dost thou think that most of them listen [to thy message] and use their reason? Nay, they are but like cattle - nay, they are even less conscious of the right way
t.yusuf-ali: Or thinkest thou that most of them listen or understand? They are only like cattle;- nay, they are worse astray in Path
t.pickthall: Or deemest thou that most of them hear or understand? They are but as the cattle - nay, but they are farther astray
t.arberry: Or deemest thou that most of them hear or understand? They are but as the cattle; nay, they are further astray from the way
t.hilali-khan: Or do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are only like cattle; nay, they are even farther astray from the Path. (i.e. even worst than cattle)
t.itani: Or do you assume that most of them hear or understand? They are just like cattle, but even more errant in their way.
t.mubarakpuri: Or do you think that most of them hear or understand They are only like cattle -- nay, they are even farther astray from the path.
t.qarib: And do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are just like cattle; indeed, they are [even] more lost
t.wahiduddin: Do you think most of them can hear or understand? They are like cattle. Indeed, they are even more astray
t.shakir: Or do you think that most of them do hear or understand? They are nothing but as cattle; nay, they are straying farther off from the path
t.haleem: Do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are just like cattle- no, they are further from the path
t.maariful: Or do you think that most of them listen or understand? They are but like cattle. Rather, they are even farther astray from the (right) way
t.khattab: Or do you think that most of them listen or understand? They are only like cattle—no, more than that, they are astray from the ˹Right˺ Way
t.ghali: Or even do you reckon that most of them hear or consider? Decidedly they are (nothing) except as the cattle. (Ancam inclues cattle, camels, sheep and goats) No indeed, they have erred further from the way
t.irving: Or do you reckon that most of them do hear or even use their reason? They are just like livestock-indeed, they are even further off the track
t.iwq: Or do you think that most of them hear or reason? They are not except like livestock. Rather, they are even more astray in their way

t_id.kemenag: atau apakah kamu mengira bahwa kebanyakan mereka itu mendengar atau memahami. Mereka itu tidak lain, hanyalah seperti binatang ternak, bahkan mereka lebih sesat jalannya (dari binatang ternak itu).
t_id.sabiq: Apakah kamu mengira bahwa kebanyakan mereka telah mendengar dengan seksama dan menggunakan akal mereka untuk mendapatkan petunjuk? Sesungguhnya mereka hanyalah mengikuti perintah mimpi- mimpi kosong mereka. Mereka telah menjadi seperti binatang ternak yang tidak memiliki keinginan sama sekali selain makan, minum dan mengejar kesenangan duniawi. Mereka tidak berfikir apa-apa yang ada di balik itu semua. Bahkan posisi mereka lebih jelek dari binatang ternak. Sebab, binatang ternak tunduk mengikuti tuannya yang menggiring kepada kebaikan dan menjauhkannya dari yang mencelakakannya. Sementara mereka mencampakkan diri mereka kepada kehancuran.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau apakah kamu mengira bahwa kebanyakan mereka itu mendengar) dengan pendengaran yang dibarengi dengan pengertian (atau memahami) apa yang kamu katakan kepada mereka. (Tiada lain) (mereka itu hanyalah seperti binatang ternak, bahkan mereka lebih sesat lagi jalannya) daripada binatang ternak itu, karena binatang ternak mau menurut dan patuh kepada penggembalanya, sedangkan mereka tidak mau menaati Pemeliharanya, yaitu Allah, yang telah memberikan kenikmatan kepada mereka.
t_id.iwq: atau apakah kamu mengira bahwa kebanyakan mereka itu mendengar atau memahami. Mereka itu tidak lain, hanyalah seperti binatang ternak, bahkan mereka lebih sesat jalannya (dari binatang ternak itu).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم تحسب أن أكثرهم يسمعون» سماع تفهم «أو يعقلون» ما تقول لهم «إن» ما «هم إلا كالأنعام بل هم أضل سبيلا» أخطأ طريقا منها لأنها تنقاد لمن يتعهدها، وهم لا يطيعون مولاهم المنعم عليهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ تَحۡسَبُ أَنَّ أَكۡثَرَهُمۡ يَسۡمَعُونَ أَوۡ يَعۡقِلُونَۚ إِنۡ هُمۡ إِلَّا كَٱلۡأَنۡعَٰمِ بَلۡ هُمۡ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau apakah kamu mengira bahwa kebanyakan mereka itu mendengar) dengan pendengaran yang dibarengi dengan pengertian (atau memahami) apa yang kamu katakan kepada mereka. (Tiada lain) (mereka itu hanyalah seperti binatang ternak, bahkan mereka lebih sesat lagi jalannya) daripada binatang ternak itu, karena binatang ternak mau menurut dan patuh kepada penggembalanya, sedangkan mereka tidak mau menaati Pemeliharanya, yaitu Allah, yang telah memberikan kenikmatan kepada mereka

ar: أَلَمۡ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَیۡفَ مَدَّ ٱلظِّلَّ وَلَوۡ شَاۤءَ لَجَعَلَهُۥ سَاكِنࣰا ثُمَّ جَعَلۡنَا ٱلشَّمۡسَ عَلَیۡهِ دَلِیلࣰا
ar_simple: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيْفَ مَدَّ الظِّلَّ وَلَوْ شَاءَ لَجَعَلَهُ سَاكِنًا ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا الشَّمْسَ عَلَيْهِ دَلِيلًا

t.sahih-int: Have you not considered your Lord - how He extends the shadow, and if He willed, He could have made it stationary? Then We made the sun for it an indication
t.asad: Art thou not aware of thy Sustainer [through His works]? - how He causes the shadow to lengthen [towards the night] when, had He so willed, He could indeed have made it stand still: but then, We have made the sun its guide
t.yusuf-ali: Hast thou not turned thy vision to thy Lord?- How He doth prolong the shadow! If He willed, He could make it stationary! then do We make the sun its guide
t.pickthall: Hast thou not seen how thy Lord hath spread the shade - And if He willed He could have made it still - then We have made the sun its pilot
t.arberry: Hast thou not regarded thy Lord, how He has stretched out the shadow? Had He willed, He would have made it still. Then We appointed the sun, to be a guide to it
t.hilali-khan: Have you not seen how your Lord spread the shadow. If He willed, He could have made it still then We have made the sun its guide [i.e. after the sunrise, it (the shadow) squeezes and vanishes at midnoon and then again appears in the afternoon with the decline of the sun, and had there been no sun light, there would have been no shadow
t.itani: Do you not see how your Lord extends the shadow? Had He willed, He could have made it still. And We made the sun a pointer to it.
t.mubarakpuri: Have you not seen how your Lord spread the shadow. If He willed, He could have made it still -- but We have made the sun its guide.
t.qarib: Have you not considered how your Lord lengthens the shadow [when], had He so willed, He could have made it stationary? [Instead], We have made the sun its guide
t.wahiduddin: Have you not seen how your Lord lengthens the shadows? Had He pleased, He could have made them constant; then We placed the sun as an indicator for them
t.shakir: Have you not considered (the work of) your Lord, how He extends the shade? And if He had pleased He would certainly have made it stationary; then We have made the sun an indication of it
t.haleem: Do you not see how your Lord lengthens the shade? If He had willed, He could have made it stand still- We made the sun its indicator
t.maariful: Have you not observed how your Lord stretched out the shadow (of the sun)? If He so willed, He would have made it stand still. Then We made the sun an indicator for it
t.khattab: Have you not seen how your Lord extends the shade—He could have simply made it ˹remain˺ still if He so willed—then We make the sun its guide
t.ghali: Have you not regarded your Lord, how He has extended the shade? And if He had so decided, He would indeed have made it still. Thereafter We made the sun a constant indicator of it
t.irving: Have you not seen how your Lord lengthens shadows? If He so wished, He would make them stand still. Then We placed the sun as an indicator for them
t.iwq: Have you not considered your Lord - how He extends the shadow, and if He willed, He could have made it stationary? Then We made the sun for it an indication

t_id.kemenag: Apakah kamu tidak memperhatikan (penciptaan) Tuhanmu, bagaimana Dia memanjangkan (dan memendekkan) bayang-bayang dan kalau Dia menghendaki niscaya Dia menjadikan tetap bayang-bayang itu, kemudian Kami jadikan matahari sebagai petunjuk atas bayang-bayang itu,
t_id.sabiq: Kami telah memberikan bukti-bukti keesaan Tuhan yang dapat digunakan oleh orang-orang yang mau menggunakan fikiran. Cobalah perhatikan bayang-bayang. Allah telah membentangkannya pada pagi hari dan menjadikannya tenang. Dengan munculnya matahari, bagian bayang-bayang yang terkena sinar menjadi hilang. Maka dengan demikian matahari menjadi bukti adanya bayang-bayang. Kalau tidak karena matahari, niscaya bayang-bayang itu tidak dapat diketahui. Kalau Allah menghendaki, Dia akan menjadikan bayang-bayang itu menyelimuti manusia, sehingga dengan begitu beberapa keperluan manusia menjadi terlewat(1). (1) Ayat ini menyinggung salah satu bukti kekuasaan dan pemeliharaan Sang Pencipta. Panjang dan pendek yang terjadi pada bayangan menunjukkan adanya proses perputaran bumi--baik pada porosnya maupun mengelilingi matahari--dalam posisi miring. Jika dua proses perputaran itu tidak ada, bayangan akan diam, karena matahari hanya menyinari salah satu paroan bumi saja, sedangkan paroan yang lain akan gelap dan malam sepanjang tahun. Akibatnya, keseimbangan suhu udara menjadi rusak dan kehidupan menjadi tidak mungkin. Selanjutnya, hal itu juga bisa terjadi apabila tempo gerak bumi pada porosnya (rotasi) berbanding lurus dengan tempo gerak bumi mengelilingi matahari (revolusi). Dengan demikian, satu hari sama dengan satu tahun. Tidak ada yang dapat melakukan hal seperti itu kecuali Allah, di samping bayangan itu sendiri adalah salah satu karunia Allah. Seandainya Allah menjadikan semua benda menjadi bening atau tembus pandang, maka bayangan tidak akan ada dan kehidupan menjadi tidak mungkin.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Apakah kamu tidak memperhatikan) yakni tidak melihat (kepada) ciptaan (Rabbmu, bagaimana Dia memanjangkan bayang-bayang) mulai dari tenggelamnya matahari sampai dengan hendak terbitnya (dan kalau Dia menghendaki) yakni Rabbmu (niscaya Dia menjadikan bayang-bayang itu tetap) artinya tidak hilang sekalipun matahari terbit (kemudian Kami jadikan matahari atasnya) yakni bayang-bayang atau gelap itu (sebagai petunjuk) karena seandainya tidak ada matahari maka niscaya bayang-bayang atau gelap itu tidak akan dikenal.
t_id.iwq: Apakah kamu tidak memperhatikan (penciptaan) Tuhanmu, bagaimana Dia memanjangkan (dan memendekkan) bayang-bayang dan kalau Dia menghendaki niscaya Dia menjadikan tetap bayang-bayang itu, kemudian Kami jadikan matahari sebagai petunjuk atas bayang-bayang itu,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ألم تَرَ» تنظر «إلى» فعل «ربّك كيف مدَّ الظل» من وقت الإسفار إلى وقت طلوع الشمس «ولو شاء» ربك «لجعله ساكنا» مقيما لا يزول بطلوع الشمس «ثم جعلنا الشمس عليه» أي الظل «دليلا» فلولا الشمس ما عرف الظل
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَلَمۡ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيۡفَ مَدَّ ٱلظِّلَّ وَلَوۡ شَآءَ لَجَعَلَهُۥ سَاكِنٗا ثُمَّ جَعَلۡنَا ٱلشَّمۡسَ عَلَيۡهِ دَلِيلٗا (مَدَّ الظِّلَّ: بَسَطَهُ مِنْ طُلُوعِ الفَجْرِ إِلَى طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ, سَاكِنًا: ثَابِتًا مُسْتَقِرًّا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Apakah kamu tidak memperhatikan) yakni tidak melihat (kepada) ciptaan (Rabbmu, bagaimana Dia memanjangkan bayang-bayang) mulai dari tenggelamnya matahari sampai dengan hendak terbitnya (dan kalau Dia menghendaki) yakni Rabbmu (niscaya Dia menjadikan bayang-bayang itu tetap) artinya tidak hilang sekalipun matahari terbit (kemudian Kami jadikan matahari atasnya) yakni bayang-bayang atau gelap itu (sebagai petunjuk) karena seandainya tidak ada matahari maka niscaya bayang-bayang atau gelap itu tidak akan dikenal

ar: ثُمَّ قَبَضۡنَـٰهُ إِلَیۡنَا قَبۡضࣰا یَسِیرࣰا
ar_simple: ثُمَّ قَبَضْنَاهُ إِلَيْنَا قَبْضًا يَسِيرًا

t.sahih-int: Then We hold it in hand for a brief grasp
t.asad: and then, [after having caused it to lengthen,] We draw it in towards Ourselves with a gradual drawing-in
t.yusuf-ali: Then We draw it in towards Ourselves,- a contraction by easy stages
t.pickthall: Then We withdraw it unto Us, a gradual withdrawal
t.arberry: thereafter We seize it to Ourselves, drawing it gently
t.hilali-khan: Then We withdraw it to Us a gradual concealed withdrawal
t.itani: Then We withdraw it towards Us gradually.
t.mubarakpuri: Then We withdraw it towards Ourselves -- a gradual withdrawal.
t.qarib: and We draw it back toward us gradually
t.wahiduddin: then We withdrew it to Us, a gradual withdrawal
t.shakir: Then We take it to Ourselves, taking little by little
t.haleem: but We gradually draw it towards Us, little by little
t.maariful: Then We pulled it toward Us in a gradual manner
t.khattab: causing the shade to retreat gradually
t.ghali: Thereafter We grasped it to Us, an easy (Or: gradual) grasping
t.irving: next We gradually pulled them toward Us
t.iwq: Then We hold it in hand for a brief grasp

t_id.kemenag: kemudian Kami menarik bayang-bayang itu kepada kami dengan tarikan yang perlahan-lahan.
t_id.sabiq: Kami menghilangkan bayang-bayang itu dengan matahari secara berangsur-angsur, tidak sekaligus. Dengan begitu, banyak sekali manfaat yang dirasakan manusia.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian Kami menarik bayang-bayang itu) yakni bayang-bayang yang memanjang itu (kepada Kami dengan tarikan yang perlahan-lahan), yaitu dengan terbitnya matahari.
t_id.iwq: kemudian Kami menarik bayang-bayang itu kepada kami dengan tarikan yang perlahan-lahan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ثم قبضناه» أي الظل الممدود «إلينا قبضا يسيرا» خفيفا بطلوع الشمس
tafsir.siraj-ar: ثُمَّ قَبَضۡنَٰهُ إِلَيۡنَا قَبۡضٗا يَسِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian Kami menarik bayang-bayang itu) yakni bayang-bayang yang memanjang itu (kepada Kami dengan tarikan yang perlahan-lahan), yaitu dengan terbitnya matahari

ar: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِی جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّیۡلَ لِبَاسࣰا وَٱلنَّوۡمَ سُبَاتࣰا وَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ نُشُورࣰا
ar_simple: وَهُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ لِبَاسًا وَالنَّوْمَ سُبَاتًا وَجَعَلَ النَّهَارَ نُشُورًا

t.sahih-int: And it is He who has made the night for you as clothing and sleep [a means for] rest and has made the day a resurrection
t.asad: And He it is who makes the night a garment for you, and [your] sleep a rest, and causes every [new] day to be a resurrection
t.yusuf-ali: And He it is Who makes the Night as a Robe for you, and Sleep as Repose, and makes the Day (as it were) a Resurrection
t.pickthall: And He it is Who maketh night a covering for you, and sleep repose, and maketh day a resurrection
t.arberry: It is He who appointed the night for you to be a garment and sleep for a rest, and day He appointed for a rising
t.hilali-khan: And it is He Who makes the night a covering for you, and the sleep (as) repose, and makes the day Nushur (i.e. getting up and going about here and there for daily work, etc. after one's sleep at night or like resurrection after one's death)
t.itani: And it is He who made the night a covering for you, and sleep for rest; and He made the day a revival.
t.mubarakpuri: And it is He Who makes the night a covering for you, and the sleep (as) a repose, and makes the day Nushur.
t.qarib: It is He who made the night a veil for you and sleep for rest, and the day a time for rising
t.wahiduddin: It is He who made the night a mantle for you, and sleep for repose; and made the day a time for rising
t.shakir: And He it is Who made the night a covering for you, and the sleep a rest, and He made the day to rise up again
t.haleem: It is He who made the night a garment for you, and sleep a rest, and made the day like a resurrection
t.maariful: He is the One who has made the night an apparel for you, and the sleep a means of rest, and has made the day a means of revival
t.khattab: He is the One Who has made the night for you as a cover, and ˹made˺ sleep for resting, and the day for rising
t.ghali: And He is the One Who has made the night for you (to be) a garment and sleep for repose and has made day-time for rising
t.irving: He is the One Who has placed night as a garment for you, and sleep for repose; He makes daytime for rising again
t.iwq: And it is He who has made the night for you as clothing and sleep a means for rest and has made the day a resurrection

t_id.kemenag: Dialah yang menjadikan untukmu malam (sebagai) pakaian, dan tidur untuk istirahat, dan Dia menjadikan siang untuk bangun berusaha.
t_id.sabiq: Di antara bukti-bukti keesaan Tuhan, Dia menjadikan malam dengan kegelapannya sebagai penutup. Semua makhluk larut dalam kegelapan yang menyelimutinya, seperti baju yang menyelimuti pemakainya. Dia membuat manusia bisa tidur untuk beristirahat memulihkan tenaga setelah kelelahan. Dengan datangnya siang, Dia membuat manusia bangun dan berusaha mencari penghidupan dan rezeki.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dialah yang menjadikan untuk kalian malam sebagai pakaian) yakni yang menutupi bagaikan pakaian (dan tidur untuk istirahat) bagi tubuh setelah selesai dari bekerja (dan Dia menjadikan siang untuk bangun berusaha) kalian bangun di waktu itu untuk mencari rezeki dan melakukan pekerjaan-pekerjaan lainnya.
t_id.iwq: Dialah yang menjadikan untukmu malam (sebagai) pakaian, dan tidur untuk istirahat, dan Dia menjadikan siang untuk bangun berusaha.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهو الذي جعل لكم الليل لباسا» ساترا كاللباس «والنوم سُباتا» راحة للأبدان بقطع الأعمال «وجعل النهار نشورا» منشورا فيه لابتغاء الرزق وغيره
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيۡلَ لِبَاسٗا وَٱلنَّوۡمَ سُبَاتٗا وَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ نُشُورٗا (لِبَاسًا: سَاتِرًا لَكُمْ بِظَلَامِهِ, سُبَاتًا: رَاحَةً لِأَبْدَانِكُمْ, نُشُورًا: وَقْتًا لِلِانْتِشَارِ وَالسَّعْيِ فيِ الأَرْضِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dialah yang menjadikan untuk kalian malam sebagai pakaian) yakni yang menutupi bagaikan pakaian (dan tidur untuk istirahat) bagi tubuh setelah selesai dari bekerja (dan Dia menjadikan siang untuk bangun berusaha) kalian bangun di waktu itu untuk mencari rezeki dan melakukan pekerjaan-pekerjaan lainnya

ar: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِیۤ أَرۡسَلَ ٱلرِّیَـٰحَ بُشۡرَۢا بَیۡنَ یَدَیۡ رَحۡمَتِهِۦۚ وَأَنزَلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَاۤءِ مَاۤءࣰ طَهُورࣰا
ar_simple: وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ الرِّيَاحَ بُشْرًا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ رَحْمَتِهِ ۚ وَأَنْزَلْنَا مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً طَهُورًا

t.sahih-int: And it is He who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy, and We send down from the sky pure water
t.asad: And He it is who sends forth the winds as a glad tiding of His coming grace; and [thus, too,] We cause pure water to descend from the skies
t.yusuf-ali: And He it is Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His mercy, and We send down pure water from the sky
t.pickthall: And He it is Who sendeth the winds, glad tidings heralding His mercy, and We send down purifying water from the sky
t.arberry: And it is He who has loosed the winds, bearing good tidings before His mercy; and We sent down from heaven pure water
t.hilali-khan: And it is He Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His Mercy (rain), and We send down pure water from the sky
t.itani: And it is He who sends the winds, bringing advance news of His mercy; and We send down from the sky pure water.
t.mubarakpuri: And it is He Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His mercy; and We send down pure water from the sky,
t.qarib: And it is He who sends forth the winds, bringing good news of His mercy. And We send down from the sky pure water
t.wahiduddin: It is He who sends the winds as heralds of His mercy and We send down pure water from the sky
t.shakir: And He it is Who sends the winds as good news before His mercy; and We send down pure water from the cloud
t.haleem: It is He who sends the winds as heralds of good news before His Mercy. We send down pure water from the sky
t.maariful: And He is the One who has sent the winds conveying good news before His mercy, and We have sent down purifying water from the heavens
t.khattab: And He is the One Who sends the winds ushering in His mercy, and We send down pure rain from the sky
t.ghali: And He is the One Who has sent the winds, bearing good tidings before (Literally: between the two hands of His mercy) His mercy; and We have sent down from the heaven water most pure
t.irving: He is the One Who has sent the winds as heralds announcing His mercy. We send pure water down from the sky
t.iwq: And it is He who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy, and We send down from the sky pure water

t_id.kemenag: Dialah yang meniupkan angin (sebagai) pembawa kabar gembira dekat sebelum kedatangan rahmat-nya (hujan); dan Kami turunkan dari langit air yang amat bersih,
t_id.sabiq: Allahlah yang menundukkan angin untuk menggiring awan. Angin tersebut juga sebagai pertanda berita gembira datangnya hujan yang merupakan rahmat Allah untuk manusia. Sesungguhnya Kami turunkan dari langit air yang suci dan menyucikan, serta dapat menghilangkan najis dan kotoran(1). (1) Pada ayat ini Allah memberitahukan bahwa Dia memberikan nikmat kepada manusia berupa turunnya air yang suci dari langit untuk mereka. Ayat ini menunjukkan bahwa air hujan, ketika pertama kali terbentuk, sangat bersih. Meskipun ketika turun air tersebut membawa benda-benda dan atom-atom yang ada di udara, air itu masih tetap sangat suci.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dialah yang meniupkan angin) menurut qiraat yang lain lafal Ar-Riih dibaca Ar-Riyah (pembawa kabar gembira dekat sebelum kedatangan rahmat-Nya) yakni dekat sebelum hujan. Lafal Nusyuran menurut suatu qiraat dibaca Nusyran, artinya secara terpisah-pisah yakni dibaca secara Takhfif supaya ringan bacaannya. Menurut qiraat yang lain dibaca Nasyran karena dianggap sebagai Mashdar. Menurut qiraat lainnya lagi dibaca Busyra, artinya sebagai pembawa kabar gembira. Bentuk tunggal bacaan pertama adalah Nusyurun, wazannya sama dengan lafal Rasulun yang bentuk jamaknya adalah Rusulun. Sedangkan bentuk tunggal dari bacaan yang kedua yaitu Busyran ialah Basyirun, artinya pembawa kabar gembira (dan Kami turunkan dan langit air yang amat bersih) yaitu air yang dapat dipakai untuk bersuci, atau air yang menyucikan.
t_id.iwq: Dialah yang meniupkan angin (sebagai) pembawa kabar gembira dekat sebelum kedatangan rahmat-nya (hujan); dan Kami turunkan dari langit air yang amat bersih,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهو الذي أرسل الرياح» وفي قراءة الريح «نُشرا بين يديْ رحمته» متفرقة قدام المطر، وفي قراءة بسكون الشين تخفيفا، وفي أخرى بسكونها ونون مفتوحة مصدر، وفي أخرى بسكونها وضم الموحدة بدل النون: أي مبشرات ومفرد الأولى نشور كرسول والأخيرة بشير «وأنزلنا من السماء ماءً طهورا» مطهرا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِيٓ أَرۡسَلَ ٱلرِّيَٰحَ بُشۡرَۢا بَيۡنَ يَدَيۡ رَحۡمَتِهِۦۚ وَأَنزَلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءٗ طَهُورٗا (بُشْرًا: مُبَشِّرَاتٍ بِالرَّحْمَةِ، وَهِيَ المَطَرُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dialah yang meniupkan angin) menurut qiraat yang lain lafal Ar-Riih dibaca Ar-Riyah (pembawa kabar gembira dekat sebelum kedatangan rahmat-Nya) yakni dekat sebelum hujan. Lafal Nusyuran menurut suatu qiraat dibaca Nusyran, artinya secara terpisah-pisah yakni dibaca secara Takhfif supaya ringan bacaannya. Menurut qiraat yang lain dibaca Nasyran karena dianggap sebagai Mashdar. Menurut qiraat lainnya lagi dibaca Busyra, artinya sebagai pembawa kabar gembira. Bentuk tunggal bacaan pertama adalah Nusyurun, wazannya sama dengan lafal Rasulun yang bentuk jamaknya adalah Rusulun. Sedangkan bentuk tunggal dari bacaan yang kedua yaitu Busyran ialah Basyirun, artinya pembawa kabar gembira (dan Kami turunkan dan langit air yang amat bersih) yaitu air yang dapat dipakai untuk bersuci, atau air yang menyucikan

ar: لِّنُحۡـِۧیَ بِهِۦ بَلۡدَةࣰ مَّیۡتࣰا وَنُسۡقِیَهُۥ مِمَّا خَلَقۡنَاۤ أَنۡعَـٰمࣰا وَأَنَاسِیَّ كَثِیرࣰا
ar_simple: لِنُحْيِيَ بِهِ بَلْدَةً مَيْتًا وَنُسْقِيَهُ مِمَّا خَلَقْنَا أَنْعَامًا وَأَنَاسِيَّ كَثِيرًا

t.sahih-int: That We may bring to life thereby a dead land and give it as drink to those We created of numerous livestock and men
t.asad: so that We may bring dead land to life thereby, and give to drink thereof to many [beings] of Our creation, beasts as well as humans
t.yusuf-ali: That with it We may give life to a dead land, and slake the thirst of things We have created,- cattle and men in great numbers
t.pickthall: That We may give life thereby to a dead land, and We give many beasts and men that We have created to drink thereof
t.arberry: so that We might revive a dead land, and give to drink of it, of that We created, cattle and men a many
t.hilali-khan: That We may give life thereby to a dead land, and We give to drink thereof many of the cattle and men that We had created
t.itani: To revive dead lands thereby, and to provide drink for the multitude of animals and humans We created.
t.mubarakpuri: That We may give life thereby to a dead land, and We give to drink thereof many of the cattle and men that We have created.
t.qarib: so that We may bring dead land to life and provide drink for the multitude of beings, animals and people, We have created
t.wahiduddin: so that We may bring life to a dead land; and slake the thirst of Our creation; cattle and men, in great numbers
t.shakir: That We may give life thereby to a dead land and give it for drink, out of what We have created, to cattle and many people
t.haleem: so that We can revive a dead land with it, and We give it as a drink to many animals and people We have created
t.maariful: so that We revive a dead land therewith, and give drink to the many cattle and humans created by Us
t.khattab: giving life to a lifeless land, and providing water for countless animals and humans of Our Own creation
t.ghali: That We may give life to a deceased country, and we make to drink of it, of whatever We created, many cattle and human beings
t.irving: so We may bring life to a dead land, and let everything We have created drink from it, such as livestock and men aplenty
t.iwq: That We may bring to life thereby a dead land and give it as drink to those We created of numerous livestock and men

t_id.kemenag: agar Kami menghidupkan dengan air itu negeri (tanah) yang mati, dan agar Kami memberi minum dengan air itu sebagian besar dari makhluk Kami, binatang-binatang ternak dan manusia yang banyak.
t_id.sabiq: Kami menurunkan hujan untuk menumbuhkan tanaman. Dengan hujan itu, tanah yang tadinya kering dan mati menjadi hidup. Air itu juga dapat dimanfaatkan untuk memberi minum makhluk ciptaan yang berupa binatang-binatang ternak dan manusia yang banyak.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Agar Kami menghidupkan dengan air itu negeri atau tanah yang mati) lafal Maitan dibaca Takhfif bentuk Mudzakkar dan Muannatsnya sama saja. Disebutkan dengan maksud, bahwa yang mati itu adalah tanah negeri itu (dan agar Kami memberi minum dengannya) dengan air itu (sebagian besar dari makhluk Kami, binatang-binatang ternak) unta, sapi dan kambing (dan manusia yang banyak) lafal Anaasiyyu merupakan bentuk jamak dari lafal Insaanun, bentuk asalnya adalah Anaasiinu, kemudian huruf Nun diganti menjadi Ya, lalu huruf Ya yang pertama diidgamkan kepadanya sehingga jadilah Anaasiyyu. Atau lafal Anaasiyyu ini adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Insiyyun.
t_id.iwq: agar Kami menghidupkan dengan air itu negeri (tanah) yang mati, dan agar Kami memberi minum dengan air itu sebagian besar dari makhluk Kami, binatang-binatang ternak dan manusia yang banyak.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «لنحيي به بلدة ميتا» بالتخفيف يستوي فيه المذكور والمؤنث ذكّره باعتبار المكان «ونسقيه» أي الماء «مما خلقنا أنعاما» إبلا وبقرا وغنما «وأناسيَّ كثيرا» جمع إنسان وأصله أناسين فأبدلت النون ياء وأدغمت فيها الياء أو جمع انسي
tafsir.siraj-ar: لِّنُحۡـِۧيَ بِهِۦ بَلۡدَةٗ مَّيۡتٗا وَنُسۡقِيَهُۥ مِمَّا خَلَقۡنَآ أَنۡعَٰمٗا وَأَنَاسِيَّ كَثِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Agar Kami menghidupkan dengan air itu negeri atau tanah yang mati) lafal Maitan dibaca Takhfif bentuk Mudzakkar dan Muannatsnya sama saja. Disebutkan dengan maksud, bahwa yang mati itu adalah tanah negeri itu (dan agar Kami memberi minum dengannya) dengan air itu (sebagian besar dari makhluk Kami, binatang-binatang ternak) unta, sapi dan kambing (dan manusia yang banyak) lafal Anaasiyyu merupakan bentuk jamak dari lafal Insaanun, bentuk asalnya adalah Anaasiinu, kemudian huruf Nun diganti menjadi Ya, lalu huruf Ya yang pertama diidgamkan kepadanya sehingga jadilah Anaasiyyu. Atau lafal Anaasiyyu ini adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Insiyyun

ar: وَلَقَدۡ صَرَّفۡنَـٰهُ بَیۡنَهُمۡ لِیَذَّكَّرُوا۟ فَأَبَىٰۤ أَكۡثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورࣰا
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَاهُ بَيْنَهُمْ لِيَذَّكَّرُوا فَأَبَىٰ أَكْثَرُ النَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورًا

t.sahih-int: And We have certainly distributed it among them that they might be reminded, but most of the people refuse except disbelief
t.asad: And, indeed, many times have We repeated [all] this unto men, so that they might take it to heart: but most men refuse to be aught but ingrate
t.yusuf-ali: And We have distributed the (water) amongst them, in order that they may celebrate (our) praises, but most men are averse (to aught) but (rank) ingratitude
t.pickthall: And verily We have repeated it among them that they may remember, but most of mankind begrudge aught save ingratitude
t.arberry: We have indeed turned it about amongst them, so that they may remember; yet most men refuse all but unbelief
t.hilali-khan: And indeed We have distributed it (rain or water) amongst them in order that they may remember the Grace of Allah, but most men refuse (or deny the Truth or Faith) and accept nothing but disbelief or ingratitude
t.itani: We have circulated it among them, that they may reflect, but most people persist in thanklessness.
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed We have distributed it among them in order that they may remember the grace of Allah, but most men refuse (out of) gratitude.
t.qarib: Indeed, We have distributed it among them so that they might take heed, but most people continue in their denial of the truth
t.wahiduddin: We have explained it to them in diverse ways, so that they may take heed, but most persist in their ingratitude
t.shakir: And certainly We have repeated this to them that they may be mindful, but the greater number of men do not consent to aught except denying
t.haleem: Many times We have repeated this to people so that they might take heed, but most persist in their ingratitude
t.maariful: And We have distributed it (the water) among them, so that they may take lesson. Yet most of the people refused all but ungratefulness
t.khattab: We certainly disperse it among them so they may be mindful, but most people persist in ungratefulness
t.ghali: And indeed We have already propounded it among them so that they may constantly remember; yet most mankind refuse (anything) except constant disbelief
t.irving: We have spelled it out for them so they may bear it in mind; yet most men refuse [to do] anything except disbelieve (in it)
t.iwq: And We have certainly distributed it among them that they might be reminded, but most of the people refuse except disbelief

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah mempergilirkan hujan itu diantara manusia supaya mereka mengambil pelajaran (dari padanya); maka kebanyakan manusia itu tidak mau kecuali mengingkari (nikmat).
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya al-Qur'ân ini telah Kami buat ayat-ayatnya jelas dan berulang-ulang. Yang demikian itu agar manusia selalu ingat Tuhan mereka, dapat mengambil pelajaran dan melaksanakan segala konsekuensinya. Akan tetapi kebanyakan manusia enggan dan hanya menginginkan kekafiran.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah menggilirnya) yakni air hujan itu (di antara manusia supaya mereka mengambil pelajaran daripadanya) Yadzdzakkaruu asalnya Yatadzakkaruu, kemudian huruf Ta diidgamkan kepada huruf Dzal setelah terlebih dahulu diganti menjadi Dzal pula, sehingga jadilah Yadzdzakkaruu. Menurut suatu qiraat dibaca Liyadzkuruu, sehingga artinya menjadi: supaya mereka ingat akan nikmat Allah dengan adanya air tersebut (maka kebanyakan manusia itu tidak mau kecuali mengingkari) nikmat Allah, karena mereka mengatakan bahwa hujan kita ini disebabkan munculnya bintang anu.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah mempergilirkan hujan itu diantara manusia supaya mereka mengambil pelajaran (dari padanya); maka kebanyakan manusia itu tidak mau kecuali mengingkari (nikmat).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد صرفناه» أي الماء «بينهم ليذَّكروا» أصله يتذكروا أدغمت التاء في الذال وفي قراءة ليذْكُروا بسكون الذال وضم الكاف أي نعمة الله به «فأبى أكثر الناس إلا كفورا» جحودا للنعمة حيث قالوا: مطرنا بنوء كذا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ صَرَّفۡنَٰهُ بَيۡنَهُمۡ لِيَذَّكَّرُواْ فَأَبَىٰٓ أَكۡثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورٗا (صَرَّفْنَاهُ: أَنْزَلْنَا المَطَرَ عَلَى أَرْضٍ دُونَ أُخْرَى)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah menggilirnya) yakni air hujan itu (di antara manusia supaya mereka mengambil pelajaran daripadanya) Yadzdzakkaruu asalnya Yatadzakkaruu, kemudian huruf Ta diidgamkan kepada huruf Dzal setelah terlebih dahulu diganti menjadi Dzal pula, sehingga jadilah Yadzdzakkaruu. Menurut suatu qiraat dibaca Liyadzkuruu, sehingga artinya menjadi: supaya mereka ingat akan nikmat Allah dengan adanya air tersebut (maka kebanyakan manusia itu tidak mau kecuali mengingkari) nikmat Allah, karena mereka mengatakan bahwa hujan kita ini disebabkan munculnya bintang anu

ar: وَلَوۡ شِئۡنَا لَبَعَثۡنَا فِی كُلِّ قَرۡیَةࣲ نَّذِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَبَعَثْنَا فِي كُلِّ قَرْيَةٍ نَذِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And if We had willed, We could have sent into every city a warner
t.asad: Now had We so willed. We could have [continued as before and] raised up a [separate] warner in every single community
t.yusuf-ali: Had it been Our Will, We could have sent a warner to every centre of population
t.pickthall: If We willed, We could raise up a warner in every village
t.arberry: If We had willed, We would have raised up in every city a warner
t.hilali-khan: And had We willed, We would have raised a warner in every town
t.itani: Had We willed, We could have sent to every town a warner.
t.mubarakpuri: And had We willed, We would have raised a warner in every town.
t.qarib: And if We had willed, We could have sent a warner to every town
t.wahiduddin: If We had so wished, We might have sent a warner into every town
t.shakir: And if We had pleased We would certainly have raised a warner in every town
t.haleem: If it had been Our will, We would have sent a warner to every town
t.maariful: Had We so willed, We would have sent a (separate) warner for every town, (but, according to Our wisdom, We have sent Muhammad as a prophet for all these towns)
t.khattab: Had We willed, We could have easily sent a warner to every society
t.ghali: And if We had so decided We would indeed have sent forth in every town a constant warner
t.irving: If We had so wished, We might have dispatched a warner into every town
t.iwq: And if We had willed, We could have sent into every city a warner

t_id.kemenag: Dan andaikata Kami menghendaki benar-benarlah Kami utus pada tiap-tiap negeri seorang yang memberi peringatan (rasul).
t_id.sabiq: Kalau Kami menghendaki, niscaya akan Kami kirim ke setiap negeri seorang pemberi peringatan. Maka bersungguh-sungguhlah dalam berdakwah. Jangan pedulikan omongan orang-orang kafir. Dan tinggalkan apa yang mereka datangkan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan andai kata Kami menghendaki, benar-benarlah Kami utus pada tiap-tiap negeri seorang yang memberi peringatan) untuk mengingatkan penduduknya, akan tetapi Kami mengutus kamu, hai Muhammad, kepada penduduk semua negeri sebagai pemberi peringatan kepada mereka semuanya, supaya pahalamu menjadi besar.
t_id.iwq: Dan andaikata Kami menghendaki benar-benarlah Kami utus pada tiap-tiap negeri seorang yang memberi peringatan (rasul).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولو شئنا لبعثنا في كل قرية نذيرا» يخوِّف أهلها ولكن بعثناك إلى أهل القرى كلها نذيرا ليعظم أجرك
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَوۡ شِئۡنَا لَبَعَثۡنَا فِي كُلِّ قَرۡيَةٖ نَّذِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan andai kata Kami menghendaki, benar-benarlah Kami utus pada tiap-tiap negeri seorang yang memberi peringatan) untuk mengingatkan penduduknya, akan tetapi Kami mengutus kamu, hai Muhammad, kepada penduduk semua negeri sebagai pemberi peringatan kepada mereka semuanya, supaya pahalamu menjadi besar

ar: فَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلۡكَـٰفِرِینَ وَجَـٰهِدۡهُم بِهِۦ جِهَادࣰا كَبِیرࣰا
ar_simple: فَلَا تُطِعِ الْكَافِرِينَ وَجَاهِدْهُمْ بِهِ جِهَادًا كَبِيرًا

t.sahih-int: So do not obey the disbelievers, and strive against them with the Qur'an a great striving
t.asad: hence, do not defer to [the likes and dislikes of] those who deny the truth, but strive hard against them, by means of this [divine writ], with utmost striving
t.yusuf-ali: Therefore listen not to the Unbelievers, but strive against them with the utmost strenuousness, with the (Qur'an)
t.pickthall: So obey not the disbelievers, but strive against them herewith with a great endeavour
t.arberry: So obey not the unbelievers, but struggle with them thereby mightily
t.hilali-khan: So obey not the disbelievers, but strive against them (by preaching) with the utmost endeavour, with it (the Quran)
t.itani: So do not obey the disbelievers, but strive against them with it, a mighty struggle.
t.mubarakpuri: So obey not the disbelievers, but strive against them with the utmost endeavor with it.
t.qarib: so do not go along with the unbelievers, but strove hard against them with this [Qur'an]
t.wahiduddin: so do not yield to those who deny the truth, but strive with the utmost strenuousness by means of this [Quran, to convey its message to them]
t.shakir: So do not follow the unbelievers, and strive against them a mighty striving with it
t.haleem: so do not give in to the disbelievers: strive hard against them with this Quran
t.maariful: So, (O Prophet,) do not obey the infidels, and strive against them with it (the Qur’ān), in utmost endeavor
t.khattab: So do not yield to the disbelievers, but strive diligently against them with this ˹Quran˺
t.ghali: So do not obey the disbelievers, and strive with them thereby with a constantly great striving
t.irving: Do not obey disbelievers and struggle seriously with them concerning it
t.iwq: Thus do not obey the disbelievers, and strive against them with the Qur'an a great striving

t_id.kemenag: Maka janganlah kamu mengikuti orang-orang kafir, dan berjihadlah terhadap mereka dengan Al Quran dengan jihad yang besar.
t_id.sabiq: Teruskanlah dakwahmu kepada kebenaran dan dalam menyampaikan risalah Tuhanmu. Apabila mereka menentang misi dakwahmu dan menyakiti orang-orang Mukmin, maka perangilah mereka. Berjuanglah untuk itu dengan sekuat tenaga.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka janganlah kamu mengikuti orang-orang kafir) memperturutkan hawa nafsu mereka (dan berjihadlah terhadap mereka dengannya) dengan Alquran (dengan jihad yang besar).
t_id.iwq: Maka janganlah kamu mengikuti orang-orang kafir, dan berjihadlah terhadap mereka dengan Al Al-Quran dengan jihad yang besar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلا تُطع الكافرين» في هواهم «وجاهدهم به» أي القرآن «جهادا كبيرا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ وَجَٰهِدۡهُم بِهِۦ جِهَادٗا كَبِيرٗا (وَجَاهِدْهُم بِهِ: بَلِّغْهُمُ القُرْآنَ بَاذِلًا وُسْعَكَ, جِهَادًا كَبِيرًا: لَا يُخِالِطُهُ فُتُورٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka janganlah kamu mengikuti orang-orang kafir) memperturutkan hawa nafsu mereka (dan berjihadlah terhadap mereka dengannya) dengan Alquran (dengan jihad yang besar)

ar: ۞ وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِی مَرَجَ ٱلۡبَحۡرَیۡنِ هَـٰذَا عَذۡبࣱ فُرَاتࣱ وَهَـٰذَا مِلۡحٌ أُجَاجࣱ وَجَعَلَ بَیۡنَهُمَا بَرۡزَخࣰا وَحِجۡرࣰا مَّحۡجُورࣰا
ar_simple: ۞ وَهُوَ الَّذِي مَرَجَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ هَٰذَا عَذْبٌ فُرَاتٌ وَهَٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌ وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَهُمَا بَرْزَخًا وَحِجْرًا مَحْجُورًا

t.sahih-int: And it is He who has released [simultaneously] the two seas, one fresh and sweet and one salty and bitter, and He placed between them a barrier and prohibiting partition
t.asad: AND HE it is who has given freedom of movement to the two great bodies of water – the one sweet and thirst-allaying, and the other salty and bitter - and yet has wrought between them a barrier and a forbidding ban
t.yusuf-ali: It is He Who has let free the two bodies of flowing water: One palatable and sweet, and the other salt and bitter; yet has He made a barrier between them, a partition that is forbidden to be passed
t.pickthall: And He it is Who hath given independence to the two seas (though they meet); one palatable, sweet, and the other saltish, bitter; and hath set a bar and a forbidding ban between them
t.arberry: And it is He who let forth the two seas, this one Sweet, grateful to taste, and this salt, bitter to the tongue, and He set between them a barrier, and a ban forbidden
t.hilali-khan: And it is He Who has let free the two seas (kinds of water), one palatable and sweet, and the other salt and bitter, and He has set a barrier and a complete partition between them
t.itani: And it is He who merged the two seas; this one fresh and sweet, and that one salty and bitter; and He placed between them a barrier, and an impassable boundary.
t.mubarakpuri: And it is He Who has let free the two seas, this is palatable and sweet, and that is salty and bitter; and He has set a barrier and a complete partition between them.
t.qarib: He is the one who has merged the two seas, one fresh and sweet, the other salty and bitter, and He placed between them an insurmountable barrier
t.wahiduddin: It is He who released the two bodies of flowing water, one sweet and fresh and the other salty and bitter, and set up an insurmountable barrier between them
t.shakir: And He it is Who has made two seas to flow freely, the one sweet that subdues thirst by its sweetness, and the other salt that burns by its saltness; and between the two He has made a barrier and inviolable obstruction
t.haleem: It is He who released the two bodies of flowing water, one sweet and fresh and the other salty and bitter, and put an insurmountable barrier between them
t.maariful: He is the One who joined the two seas, so as this is sweet, very sweet, and this is bitter, very bitter, and made between them a buffer and an insurmountable barrier
t.khattab: And He is the One Who merges the two bodies of water: one fresh and palatable and the other salty and bitter, placing between them a barrier they cannot cross
t.ghali: And He is the One Who has merged the two seas, this one sweet, grateful (to taste), and this salt, bitter (to the tongue); and He has made between them an isthmus, and an utter- obstruction. (Literally: obstruction obstructed)
t.irving: He is the One Who has cut off both seas, this one being sweet, fresh, while the other is salty, briny. He has placed an isthmus in between them plus a barrier to block them off
t.iwq: And it is He who has released simultaneously the two seas, one fresh and sweet and one salty and bitter, and He placed between them a barrier and prohibiting partition

t_id.kemenag: Dan Dialah yang membiarkan dua laut yang mengalir (berdampingan); yang ini tawar lagi segar dan yang lain asin lagi pahit; dan Dia jadikan antara keduanya dinding dan batas yang menghalangi.
t_id.sabiq: Allahlah yang mengalirkan air dua lautan, laut tawar dan laut asin. Masing-masing aliran saling berdampingan satu dengan lainnya. Meskipun dimikian, kedua air itu tidak tercampur. Semua itu diciptakan sebagai nikmat dan rahmat untuk manusia(1). (1) Ayat ini menunjukkan sebuah nikmat Allah kepada hamba-hamba-Nya, yaitu berupa tidak bercampurnya air asin yang merembes atau mengalir dari lautan ke batu-batuan di dekat pantai dengan air tawar yang merembes atau mengalir ke laut dari daratan. Keduanya hanya sekadar bertemu. Air tawar itu tergenang di atas air asin seakan-akan di antara keduanya ada pemisah yang menghalangi bertemunya satu dengan yang lainnya. "Hijran Mahjûran" diartikan 'pembatas yang tersembunyi yang tidak dapat kita lihat'. Bukan hanya itu, bahkan di situ juga terdapat hukum yang bersifat konstan yang mengatur hubungan tersebut untuk kepentingan manusia yang tinggal di sekitar tempat itu dan sangat tergantung kepada air tawar.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Dialah yang membiarkan dua laut mengalir) secara berdampingan (yang ini tawar lagi segar) sangat tawar lagi menyegarkan (dan yang lain asin lagi pahit) asin sekali sehingga rasanya pahit (dan Dia jadikan antara keduanya dinding) batas yang menyebabkan kedua air tersebut tidak membaur, antara yang satu dengan yang lainnya (dan batas yang menghalangi) yakni penghalang yang mencegah keduanya untuk bercampur.
t_id.iwq: Dan Dialah yang membiarkan doa laut yang mengalir (berdampingan); yang ini tawar lagi segar dan yang lain asin lagi pahit; dan Dia jadikan antara keduanya dinding dan batas yang menghalangi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهو الذي مرج البحرين» أرسلهما متجاورين «هذا عذبٌ فرات» شديد العذوبة «وهذا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌ» شديد الملوحةِ «وجعل بينهما برزخا» حاجزا لا يختلط أحدهما بالآخر «وحجرا محجورا» سترا ممنوعا به اختلاطهما
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي مَرَجَ ٱلۡبَحۡرَيۡنِ هَٰذَا عَذۡبٞ فُرَاتٞ وَهَٰذَا مِلۡحٌ أُجَاجٞ وَجَعَلَ بَيۡنَهُمَا بَرۡزَخٗا وَحِجۡرٗا مَّحۡجُورٗا (مَرَجَ: خَلَطَ, فُرَاتٌ: شَدِيدُ العُذُوبَةِ, أُجَاجٌ: شَدِيدُ المُلُوحَةِ, بَرْزَخًا: حَاجِزًا يَمْنَعُ إِفْسَادَ أَحَدِهِمَا لِلآخَرِ, وَحِجْرًا مَّحْجُورًا: سِتْرًا يَمْنَعُ وُصُولَ أَحَدِهِمَا إِلَى الآخَرِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Dialah yang membiarkan dua laut mengalir) secara berdampingan (yang ini tawar lagi segar) sangat tawar lagi menyegarkan (dan yang lain asin lagi pahit) asin sekali sehingga rasanya pahit (dan Dia jadikan antara keduanya dinding) batas yang menyebabkan kedua air tersebut tidak membaur, antara yang satu dengan yang lainnya (dan batas yang menghalangi) yakni penghalang yang mencegah keduanya untuk bercampur

ar: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِی خَلَقَ مِنَ ٱلۡمَاۤءِ بَشَرࣰا فَجَعَلَهُۥ نَسَبࣰا وَصِهۡرࣰاۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ قَدِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ مِنَ الْمَاءِ بَشَرًا فَجَعَلَهُ نَسَبًا وَصِهْرًا ۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ قَدِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And it is He who has created from water a human being and made him [a relative by] lineage and marriage. And ever is your Lord competent [concerning creation]
t.asad: And He it is who out of this [very] water has created man, and has endowed him with [the consciousness of] descent and marriage-tie: for thy Sustainer is ever infinite in His power
t.yusuf-ali: It is He Who has created man from water: then has He established relationships of lineage and marriage: for thy Lord has power (over all things)
t.pickthall: And He it is Who hath created man from water, and hath appointed for him kindred by blood and kindred by marriage; for thy Lord is ever Powerful
t.arberry: And it is He who created of water a mortal, and made him kindred of blood and marriage; thy Lord is All-powerful
t.hilali-khan: And it is He Who has created man from water, and has appointed for him kindred by blood, and kindred by marriage. And your Lord is Ever All-Powerful to do what He will
t.itani: And it is He who, from fluid, created the human being. Then He made relationships through marriage and mating. Your Lord is Omnipotent.
t.mubarakpuri: And it is He Who has created man from water, and has appointed for him kindred by blood, and kindred by marriage. And your Lord is Ever All-Powerful to do what He wills.
t.qarib: He created human beings from water and made them kin by blood and marriage. Your Lord is able to do all things
t.wahiduddin: It is He who has created human beings from water and He has granted them the ties of blood as well as marriage. Your Lord is all powerful
t.shakir: And He it is Who has created man from the water, then He has made for him blood relationship and marriage relationship, and your Lord is powerful
t.haleem: It is He who creates human beings from fluid, then makes them kin by blood and marriage: your Lord is all powerful
t.maariful: And He is the One who created man from water, then made of him relations created by lineage and relations created by marriage. Your Lord is All-Powerful
t.khattab: And He is the One Who creates human beings from a ˹humble˺ liquid, then establishes for them bonds of kinship and marriage. For your Lord is Most Capable
t.ghali: And He is The One Who created of water a mortal; so He has made him related by blood and marriage; and your Lord has been Ever-Determiner
t.irving: He is the One Who created humanity out of water; and He has granted them blood ties as well as in-laws. Your Lord has been s Capable
t.iwq: And it is He who has created from water a human being and made him a relative by lineage and marriage. And ever is your Lord competent concerning creation

t_id.kemenag: Dan Dia (pula) yang menciptakan manusia dari air lalu dia jadikan manusia itu (punya) keturunan dan mushaharah dan adalah Tuhanmu Maha Kuasa.
t_id.sabiq: Allahlah yang telah menciptakan mereka dari setetes air. Kemudian Allah menjadikan mereka laki-laki dan perempuan yang mempunyai hubungan kekerabatan melalui keturunan atau perkawinan. Allah Mahakuasa atas setiap yang dikehendaki-Nya. Sebab melalui setetes air, Dia mampu menjadikan dua jenis manusia yang berbeda.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Dia pula yang menciptakan manusia dari air) yakni dari air mani; lafal Basyar adalah sinonim dari lafal Insaan (lalu Dia jadikan manusia itu punya keturunan) punya hubungan nasab (dan mushaharah) punya hubungan mushaharah, misalnya seorang lelaki atau perempuan melakukan perkawinan dengan pasangannya untuk memperoleh keturunan, maka hubungan kekeluargaan dari perkawinan ini dinamakan hubungan Mushaharah (dan adalah Rabbmu Maha Kuasa) untuk menciptakan apa yang dikehendaki-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Dan Dia (pula) yang menciptakan manusia dari air lalu dia jadikan manusia itu (punya) keturunan dan mushaharah dan adalah Tuhanmu Maha Kuasa.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهو الذي خلق من الماء بشرا» من المني إنسانا «فجعله نسبا» ذا نسب «وصهرا» ذا صهر بأن يتزوج ذكرا كان أو أنثي طلبا للتناسل «وكان ربُّك قديرا» قادرا على ما يشاء
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ مِنَ ٱلۡمَآءِ بَشَرٗا فَجَعَلَهُۥ نَسَبٗا وَصِهۡرٗاۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ قَدِيرٗا (الْمَاءِ: مَنِيِّ الرَّجُلِ وَالمَرْأَةِ, نَسَبًا: قَرَابَةَ النَّسَبِ, وَصِهْرًا: قَرَابَةَ المُصَاهَرَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Dia pula yang menciptakan manusia dari air) yakni dari air mani; lafal Basyar adalah sinonim dari lafal Insaan (lalu Dia jadikan manusia itu punya keturunan) punya hubungan nasab (dan mushaharah) punya hubungan mushaharah, misalnya seorang lelaki atau perempuan melakukan perkawinan dengan pasangannya untuk memperoleh keturunan, maka hubungan kekeluargaan dari perkawinan ini dinamakan hubungan Mushaharah (dan adalah Rabbmu Maha Kuasa) untuk menciptakan apa yang dikehendaki-Nya

ar: وَیَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا یَنفَعُهُمۡ وَلَا یَضُرُّهُمۡۗ وَكَانَ ٱلۡكَافِرُ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ ظَهِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنْفَعُهُمْ وَلَا يَضُرُّهُمْ ۗ وَكَانَ الْكَافِرُ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِ ظَهِيرًا

t.sahih-int: But they worship rather than Allah that which does not benefit them or harm them, and the disbeliever is ever, against his Lord, an assistant [to Satan]
t.asad: And yet, some people worship, instead of God, things that can neither benefit them nor harm them: thus, he who denies the truth does indeed turn his back on his Sustainer
t.yusuf-ali: Yet do they worship, besides Allah, things that can neither profit them nor harm them: and the Misbeliever is a helper (of Evil), against his own Lord
t.pickthall: Yet they worship instead of Allah that which can neither benefit them nor hurt them. The disbeliever was ever a partisan against his Lord
t.arberry: And they serve, apart from God, what neither profits them nor hurts them; and the unbeliever is ever a partisan against his Lord
t.hilali-khan: And they (disbelievers, polytheists, etc.) worship besides Allah, that which can neither profit them nor harm them, and the disbeliever is ever a helper (of the Satan) against his Lord
t.itani: And yet, instead of God, they serve what neither profits them nor harms them. The disbeliever has always turned his back on his Lord.
t.mubarakpuri: And they worship besides Allah, that which can neither profit them nor harm them; and the disbeliever is ever a helper against his Lord.
t.qarib: Yet, instead of God, they worship what can neither benefit nor harm them. The unbeliever is always biased, against his Lord
t.wahiduddin: Yet they worship besides God that which can neither benefit them nor harm them. One who denies the truth is a helper [of evil] against his Lord
t.shakir: And they serve besides Allah that which neither profits them nor causes them harm; and the unbeliever is a partisan against his Lord
t.haleem: Yet instead of God they worship things that can neither benefit nor harm them: the disbeliever has always turned his back on his Lord
t.maariful: They worship, instead of Allah, what can neither give them any benefit nor can cause them any harm. The disbeliever is always a supporter (of Satan) against his Lord
t.khattab: Yet they worship besides Allah what can neither benefit nor harm them. And the disbeliever always collaborates against their Lord
t.ghali: And they worship, apart from Allah, what neither profits them nor harms them; and the disbeliever has been a backer against his Lord
t.irving: Yet instead of God they worship something that neither benefits nor hurts them. The disbeliever will be a backer [for anything against his Lord]
t.iwq: But they worship rather than Allah that which does not benefit them or harm them, and the disbeliever is ever, against his Lord, an assistant to Satan

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka menyembah selain Allah apa yang tidak memberi manfaat kepada mereka dan tidak (pula) memberi mudharat kepada mereka. Adalah orang-orang kafir itu penolong (syaitan untuk berbuat durhaka) terhadap Tuhannya.
t_id.sabiq: Setelah datangnya bukti-bukti yang menunjukkan bahwa hanya Allahlah yang pantas disembah, tidak selain-Nya, sebagian manusia masih ada yang menyembah berhala-berhala yang tidak dapat mendatangkan manfaat dan bahaya. Dengan keyakinannya itu, mereka berarti telah menolong setan. Padahal setan itu bermaksud menyesatkan mereka. Mereka menolak kebenaran yang diserukan oleh Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka menyembah) yakni orang-orang kafir (selain Allah apa yang tidak memberi manfaat kepada mereka) dengan menyembahnya (dan tidak pula memberi mudarat kepada mereka) jika tidak disembah, yang dimaksud adalah berhala-berhala. (Adalah orang kafir itu selalu menentang Rabbnya) selalu membantu setan dengan cara taat kepadanya dan menentang Rabbnya.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka menyembah selain Allah apa yang tidak memberi manfaat kepada mereka dan tidak (pula) memberi mudharat kepada mereka. Adalah orang-orang kafir itu penolong (syaitan untuk berbuat durhaka) terhadap Tuhannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ويعبدون» أي الكفار «من دون الله ما لا ينفعهم» بعبادته «ولا يضرهم» بتركها وهو الأصنام «وكان الكافر على ربه ظهيرا» معينا للشيطان بطاعته
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَيَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُهُمۡ وَلَا يَضُرُّهُمۡۗ وَكَانَ ٱلۡكَافِرُ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ ظَهِيرٗا (ظَهِيرًا: مُعِينًا لِلشَّيْطَانِ عَلَى رَبِّهِ؛ بِالشِّرْكِ، مُظَاهِرًا لَهُ فيِ المَعْصِيَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka menyembah) yakni orang-orang kafir (selain Allah apa yang tidak memberi manfaat kepada mereka) dengan menyembahnya (dan tidak pula memberi mudarat kepada mereka) jika tidak disembah, yang dimaksud adalah berhala-berhala. (Adalah orang kafir itu selalu menentang Rabbnya) selalu membantu setan dengan cara taat kepadanya dan menentang Rabbnya

ar: وَمَاۤ أَرۡسَلۡنَـٰكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرࣰا وَنَذِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner
t.asad: Yet [withal, O Prophet,] We have sent thee only as a herald of glad tidings and a warner
t.yusuf-ali: But thee We only sent to give glad tidings and admonition
t.pickthall: And We have sent thee (O Muhammad) only as a bearer of good tidings and a warner
t.arberry: We have sent thee not, except good tidings to bear, and warning
t.hilali-khan: And We have sent you (O Muhammad SAW) only as a bearer of glad tidings and a warner
t.itani: We sent you only as a herald of good news and a warner.
t.mubarakpuri: And We have sent you only as a bearer of good news and a warner.
t.qarib: We have sent you [Prophet] only to bring good news and deliver a warning
t.wahiduddin: We have sent you only as a bearer of glad tidings and as a warner
t.shakir: And We have not sent you but as a giver of good news and as a warner
t.haleem: We sent you only to give good news and warning
t.maariful: We did not send you but as a bearer of good news and as a warner
t.khattab: And We have sent you ˹O Prophet˺ only as a deliverer of good news and a warner
t.ghali: And in no way have We sent you except (as) a bearer of good tidings and a constant warner
t.irving: We have only sent you as a herald and a warner
t.iwq: And We have not sent you, O Muhammad, except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner

t_id.kemenag: Dan tidaklah Kami mengutus kamu melainkan hanya sebagai pembawa kabar gembira dan pemberi peringatan.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Muhammad, kewajibanmu hanyalah menyampaikan risalah, memberi kabar gembira berupa surga kepada orang-orang Mukmin dan memberitahukan berita duka untuk orang-orang kafir. Setelah kamu laksanakan itu semua, tidak ada lagi yang dituntut darimu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidaklah Kami mengutus kamu melainkan hanya sebagai pembawa kabar gembira) (dan pemberi peringatan) yang memperingatkan manusia tentang neraka.
t_id.iwq: Dan tidaklah Kami mengutus kamu melainkan hanya sebagai pembawa kabar gembira dan pemberi peringatan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما أرسلناك إلا مبشرا» بالجنة «ونذيرا» مخوفا من النار
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَٰكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرٗا وَنَذِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidaklah Kami mengutus kamu melainkan hanya sebagai pembawa kabar gembira) (dan pemberi peringatan) yang memperingatkan manusia tentang neraka

ar: قُلۡ مَاۤ أَسۡءَلُكُمۡ عَلَیۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍ إِلَّا مَن شَاۤءَ أَن یَتَّخِذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِیلࣰا
ar_simple: قُلْ مَا أَسْأَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِلَّا مَنْ شَاءَ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِ سَبِيلًا

t.sahih-int: Say, "I do not ask of you for it any payment - only that whoever wills might take to his Lord a way
t.asad: Say: “For this, no reward do I ask of you [- no reward] other than that he who so wills may unto his Sustainer find a way!”
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "No reward do I ask of you for it but this: that each one who will may take a (straight) Path to his Lord
t.pickthall: Say: I ask of you no reward for this, save that whoso will may choose a way unto his Lord
t.arberry: Say: 'I do not ask of you a wage for this, except for him who wishes to take to his Lord a way
t.hilali-khan: Say: "No reward do I ask of you for this (that which I have brought from my Lord and its preaching, etc.), save that whosoever wills, may take a Path to his Lord
t.itani: Say, “I ask of you no payment for this—only that whoever wills may take a path to his Lord.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "No reward do I ask of you for this, save that whosoever wills may take a path to his Lord."
t.qarib: Say, "I ask you no payment for doing this- only that whoever wills may take a path to his Lord
t.wahiduddin: Say, "I do not ask you for any recompense for this except that anyone who so wishes should take the right path to his Lord
t.shakir: Say: I do not ask you aught in return except that he who will, may take the way to his Lord
t.haleem: Say, ‘I am not asking for any reward for it, but anyone who wishes should take a path to his Lord.’
t.maariful: Say, “I do not demand from you any return, except that one should adopt the (proper) way to his Lord, if he so wills
t.khattab: Say, “I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺, but whoever wishes, let them pursue the Way to their Lord.”
t.ghali: Say, "In no way do I ask of you (any) reward for it except for him who decides to take for (himself) a way to his Lord
t.irving: SAY: "I do not ask you for any payment for it except that anyone who wishes to may accept a way unto his Lord
t.iwq: Say, "I do not ask of you for it any payment - only that whoever wills might take to his Lord a way

t_id.kemenag: Katakanlah: "Aku tidak meminta upah sedikitpun kepada kamu dalam menyampaikan risalah itu, melainkan (mengharapkan kepatuhan) orang-orang yang mau mengambil jalan kepada Tuhan nya.
t_id.sabiq: Katakan kepada mereka, "Aku tidak mengharap imbalan dan balasan dari seruanku kepada kalian untuk memeluk Islam. Aku hanya ingin salah seorang dari kalian ada yang mendapat petunjuk, menempuh jalan kebenaran dan kembali kepada Tuhannya."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! "Aku tidak meminta kepada kalian dalam menyampaikan hal ini) yakni menyampaikan apa yang aku diutus untuk menyampaikannya (upah sedikit pun, melainkan) tetapi hanya mengharapkan kepatuhan (orang-orang yang mau mengambil jalan kepada Rabbnya) dengan cara menginfakkan harta bendanya ke jalan keridaan-Nya, maka untuk hal ini aku tidak mencegahnya.
t_id.iwq: Katakanlah: "Aku tidak meminta upah sedikitpun kepada kamu dalam menyampaikan risalah itu, melainkan (mengharapkan kepatuhan) orang-orang yang mau mengambil jalan kepada Tuhan nya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قل ما أسألكم عليه» أي على تبليغ ما أرسلت به «من أجر إلا» لكن «من شاء أن يتخذ إلى ربه سبيلا» طريقا بإنفاق ماله في مرضاته تعالى فلا أمنعه من ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡ مَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ أَن يَتَّخِذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah! "Aku tidak meminta kepada kalian dalam menyampaikan hal ini) yakni menyampaikan apa yang aku diutus untuk menyampaikannya (upah sedikit pun, melainkan) tetapi hanya mengharapkan kepatuhan (orang-orang yang mau mengambil jalan kepada Rabbnya) dengan cara menginfakkan harta bendanya ke jalan keridaan-Nya, maka untuk hal ini aku tidak mencegahnya

ar: وَتَوَكَّلۡ عَلَى ٱلۡحَیِّ ٱلَّذِی لَا یَمُوتُ وَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِهِۦۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِۦ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِۦ خَبِیرًا
ar_simple: وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى الْحَيِّ الَّذِي لَا يَمُوتُ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِ خَبِيرًا

t.sahih-int: And rely upon the Ever-Living who does not die, and exalt [Allah] with His praise. And sufficient is He to be, with the sins of His servants, Acquainted
t.asad: Hence, place thy trust in the Living One who dies not, and extol His limitless glory and praise: for none is as aware as His creatures’ sins as He –
t.yusuf-ali: And put thy trust in Him Who lives and dies not; and celebrate his praise; and enough is He to be acquainted with the faults of His servants
t.pickthall: And trust thou in the Living One Who dieth not, and hymn His praise. He sufficeth as the Knower of His bondmen's sins
t.arberry: Put thy trust in the Living God, the Undying, and proclaim His praise. Sufficiently is He aware of His servants sins
t.hilali-khan: And put your trust (O Muhammad SAW) in the Ever Living One Who dies not, and glorify His Praises, and Sufficient is He as the All-Knower of the sins of His slaves
t.itani: And put your trust in the Living, the One who never dies; and celebrate His praise. He suffices as the All-Informed Knower of the faults of His creatures.
t.mubarakpuri: And put your trust in the Ever Living One Who dies not, and glorify His praises, and sufficient is He as the All-Knower of the sins of His servants,
t.qarib: Put your trust in the Everlasting who does not die, glorify Him and celebrate His praise. For it is sufficient that none knows His worshipers' sins as fully as does He
t.wahiduddin: Put your trust in the One who is the Ever-Living [God], who never dies, and glorify Him with His praise. He is fully aware of the sins of His servants
t.shakir: And rely on the Ever-living Who dies not, and celebrate His praise; and Sufficient is He as being aware of the faults of His servants
t.haleem: Put your trust in the Living [God] who never dies, and celebrate His praise. He knows the sins of His servants well enough
t.maariful: And place your trust in the Ever-Living who cannot die, and proclaim His purity along with His praise. Enough is He to be aware of the sins of His servants
t.khattab: Put your trust in the Ever-Living, Who never dies, and glorify His praises. Sufficient is He as All-Aware of the sins of His servants
t.ghali: (And put your trust in the Living Allah) Who does not die, and extol with His praise. And He suffices as The Ever-Cognizant of His bondmen's guilty deeds
t.irving: Rely on the Living One Who never dies. Hymn His praise! Sufficient is it for Him to be Informed about His servants´ offences
t.iwq: And rely upon the Ever-Living who does not die, and exalt Allah with His praise. And sufficient is He to be, with the sins of His servants, Acquainted

t_id.kemenag: Dan bertawakkallah kepada Allah yang hidup (kekal) Yang tidak mati, dan bertasbihlah dengan memuji-Nya. Dan cukuplah Dia Maha Mengetahui dosa-dosa hamba-hamba-Nya.
t_id.sabiq: Bertakwalah dalam segala urusan kalian kepada Allah, Zat yang Mahahidup yang tidak akan mati. Sucikan dan agungkanlah Dia dengan memuji segala nikmat-Nya. Biarkanlah orang yang keluar dari jalan kebenaran. Allah Maha Mengetahui ihwal mereka dan akan membalas dosa-dosa mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan bertawakallah kepada Allah Yang Hidup Kekal Yang tidak mati, dan bertasbihlah) seraya (memuji kepada-Nya) yakni, katakanlah, 'Subhanallah Wal Hamdulillah' yang artinya; Maha Suci Allah dan segala puji bagi Allah. (Dan cukuplah Dia Maha Mengetahui dosa hamba-hamba-Nya) lafal Bihi berta'alluq kepada lafal Dzunubi.
t_id.iwq: Dan bertawakkallah kepada Allah yang hidup (kekal) Yang tidak mati, dan bertasbihlah dengan memuji-Nya. Dan cukuplah Dia Maha Mengetahui dosa-dosa hamba-hamba-Nya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وتوكل على الحي الذي لا يموت وسَبِّح» متلبسا «بحمده» أي قل: سبحان الله والحمد لله «وكفى به بذنوب عباده خبيرا» عالما تعلق به بذنوب
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَتَوَكَّلۡ عَلَى ٱلۡحَيِّ ٱلَّذِي لَا يَمُوتُ وَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِهِۦۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِۦ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرًا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan bertawakallah kepada Allah Yang Hidup Kekal Yang tidak mati, dan bertasbihlah) seraya (memuji kepada-Nya) yakni, katakanlah, 'Subhanallah Wal Hamdulillah' yang artinya; Maha Suci Allah dan segala puji bagi Allah. (Dan cukuplah Dia Maha Mengetahui dosa hamba-hamba-Nya) lafal Bihi berta'alluq kepada lafal Dzunubi

ar: ٱلَّذِی خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَیۡنَهُمَا فِی سِتَّةِ أَیَّامࣲ ثُمَّ ٱسۡتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلۡعَرۡشِۖ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنُ فَسۡءَلۡ بِهِۦ خَبِیرࣰا
ar_simple: الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ۚ الرَّحْمَٰنُ فَاسْأَلْ بِهِ خَبِيرًا

t.sahih-int: He who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days and then established Himself above the Throne - the Most Merciful, so ask about Him one well informed
t.asad: He who has created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six aeons, and is established on the throne of His almightiness: the Most Gracious! Ask, then, about Him, [the] One who is [truly] aware
t.yusuf-ali: He Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between, in six days, and is firmly established on the Throne (of Authority): Allah Most Gracious: ask thou, then, about Him of any acquainted (with such things)
t.pickthall: Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days, then He mounted the Throne. The Beneficent! Ask anyone informed concerning Him
t.arberry: who created the heavens and the earth, and what between them is, in six days, then sat Himself upon the Throne, the All-compassionate: ask any informed of Him
t.hilali-khan: Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days. Then He Istawa (rose over) the Throne (in a manner that suits His Majesty). The Most Beneficent (Allah)! Ask Him (O Prophet Muhammad SAW), (concerning His Qualities, His rising over His Throne, His creations, etc.), as He is Al-Khabir (The All-Knower of everything i.e. Allah)
t.itani: He who created the heavens and the earth and everything between them in six days, then settled on the Throne. The Most Merciful. Ask about Him a well-informed.
t.mubarakpuri: Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days. Then He rose over (Istawa) the Throne. The Most Gracious! Ask Him, as He is the All-Knower.
t.qarib: Who created the heavens and the earth and everything in between them in six days, and then established Himself above the Throne of His majesty- the Merciful-to-all! Ask about Him, then, the well informed
t.wahiduddin: it is He who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days [periods], then settled Himself on the throne -- the Gracious One. Ask any informed person about Him
t.shakir: Who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six periods, and He is firmly established on the throne of authority; the Beneficent Allah, so ask respecting it one aware
t.haleem: it is He who created the heavens and earth and what is between them in six Days, and then established Himself on the throne- He is the Lord of Mercy; He is the Best Informed
t.maariful: the One who created in six days the heavens and the earth and whatever lies between them, then He positioned Himself on the Throne. (He is) the RaHmān (the All-Merciful, Allah). So ask about Him someone who knows
t.khattab: ˹He is˺ the One Who created the heavens and the earth and everything in between in six Days, then established Himself on the Throne. ˹He is˺ the Most Compassionate! Ask ˹none other than˺ the All-Knowledgeable about Himself
t.ghali: (He is the one) who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, in six days; thereafter He leveled Himself upon the Throne. (How He did so is beyond human understanding) (He is) The All-Merciful, so ask any cognizant of Him
t.irving: since He is the One Who created Heaven and Earth as well as whatever lies in between them, in six days. Then the Mercy-giving mounted on the Throne. Ask any informed (person) about Him
t.iwq: He who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days and then established Himself above the Throne - the Ever-Merciful, so ask about Him one well informed

t_id.kemenag: Yang menciptakan langit dan bumi dan apa yang ada antara keduanya dalam enam masa, kemudian dia bersemayam di atas 'Arsy, (Dialah) Yang Maha Pemurah, maka tanyakanlah (tentang Allah) kepada yang lebih mengetahui (Muhammad) tentang Dia.
t_id.sabiq: Allahlah yang menciptakan langit dan bumi beserta apa yang ada di antara keduanya dalam enam hari. Dia menguasai singgasana kerajaan. Kekuasaan-Nya meliputi segala sesuatu. Dialah Zat yang Maha Pengasih. Apabila kamu ingin tahu sifat-sifat-Nya, maka tanyakanlah kepada siapa yang mengetahui hal itu. Dia pasti akan memberikan jawaban untukmu. Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Mengetahui lagi Mahabijaksana(1). (1) Enam hari yang yang disebutkan di dalam ayat di atas merupakan ungkapan Allah tentang masa. Dialah yang lebih tahu ukuran hari-hari tersebut. Dari sudut pandang ilmu pengetahuan, proses kejadian alam melewati beberapa fase. Adapun Firman Allah yang berbunyi "…al-samâwât wa al-ardl wa mâ baynahumâ…" ('…langit, bumi dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya…') menunjukkan semua benda-benda langit yang terdiri atas bintang, matahari, planet, bulan, debu kosmos, gas dan energi yang kesemuanya membentuk alam raya. Sedangkan Firman Allah "…tsumma istawâ 'alâ al-'arsy…" ('…kemudian Dia menguasai 'arsy…') menetapkan bahwa dilihat dari segi waktu, alam ini mempunyai permulaan, dan bahwa terbentuknya alam ini disertai dengan hukum alam atau sunnatullah yang mengatur semuanya. Dengan adanya suatu sistem alam ini yang rinci dan sempurna serta mencakup segala sesuatu, penguasaan Allah terhadap alam secara global dan rinci menjadi jelas. Mengenai firman-Nya"…fa is'al bihî khabîran" ('…Tanyakanlah kepada yang mengetahui'), mengandung anjuran akan pentingnya meneliti dan menggali gejala-gejala alam dan sistem yang ada di dalamnya untuk mengetahui rahasia-rahasia kekuasaan Allah dalam penciptaan alam.
t_id.jalalayn-id: Dia adalah (Yang menciptakan langit dan bumi dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya dalam enam hari) dari hari-hari dunia menurut perkiraan; karena pada masa itu masih belum ada matahari. Akan tetapi jika Dia menghendaki niscaya Dia dapat menciptakan kesemuanya dalam waktu sekejap saja. Sengaja Dia memakai cara ini dengan maksud untuk mengajari makhluk-Nya supaya berlaku perlahan-lahan dan tidak tergesa-gesa dalam segala hal (kemudian Dia berkuasa di atas Arasy) arti kata Arasy menurut istilah bahasa adalah singgasana raja. (yakni Allah Yang Maha Penyayang) lafal Ar-Rahmaan ini berkedudukan menjadi Badal dari Dhamir yang terkandung di dalam lafal Istawaa Makna Istawaa ialah bersemayam, karena ungkapan inilah yang sesuai dengan keagungan dan kebesaran-Nya (maka tanyakanlah) hai manusia (tentang Dia) tentang Allah Yang Maha Pemurah (kepada orang yang mengetahui.") tentang-Nya, dia akan menceritakan kepada-Mu mengenai sifat-sifat-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Yang menciptakan langit dan bumi dan apa yang ada antara keduanya dalam enam masa, kemudian dia bersemayam di atas 'Arsy, (Dialah) Yang Maha Pemurah, maka tanyakanlah (tentang Allah) kepada yang lebih mengetahui (Muhammad) tentang Dia.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهو الذي خلق السماوات والأرض وما بينهما في ستة أيام» من أيام الدنيا: أي في قدرها لأنه لم يكن ثم شمس ولو شاء لخلقهنّ في لمحة والعدول عنه لتعليم خلقه التثبيت «ثم استوى على العرش» هو في اللغة سرير الملك «الرحمن» بدل من ضمير استوى: أي استواءً يليق به «فاسأل» أيها الإنسان «به» بالرحمن «خبيرا» يخبرك بصفاته
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٖ ثُمَّ ٱسۡتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلۡعَرۡشِۖ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ فَسۡـَٔلۡ بِهِۦ خَبِيرٗا (اسْتَوَى: عَلَا وَارْتَفَعَ اسْتَوَاءً يَلِيقُ بِجَلَالِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dia adalah (Yang menciptakan langit dan bumi dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya dalam enam hari) dari hari-hari dunia menurut perkiraan; karena pada masa itu masih belum ada matahari. Akan tetapi jika Dia menghendaki niscaya Dia dapat menciptakan kesemuanya dalam waktu sekejap saja. Sengaja Dia memakai cara ini dengan maksud untuk mengajari makhluk-Nya supaya berlaku perlahan-lahan dan tidak tergesa-gesa dalam segala hal (kemudian Dia berkuasa di atas Arasy) arti kata Arasy menurut istilah bahasa adalah singgasana raja. (yakni Allah Yang Maha Penyayang) lafal Ar-Rahmaan ini berkedudukan menjadi Badal dari Dhamir yang terkandung di dalam lafal Istawaa Makna Istawaa ialah bersemayam, karena ungkapan inilah yang sesuai dengan keagungan dan kebesaran-Nya (maka tanyakanlah) hai manusia (tentang Dia) tentang Allah Yang Maha Pemurah (kepada orang yang mengetahui.") tentang-Nya, dia akan menceritakan kepada-Mu mengenai sifat-sifat-Nya

ar: وَإِذَا قِیلَ لَهُمُ ٱ̅سۡ̅جُ̅دُ̅و̅ا۟̅ لِلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ قَالُوا۟ وَمَا ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنُ أَنَسۡجُدُ لِمَا تَأۡمُرُنَا وَزَادَهُمۡ نُفُورࣰا ۩
ar_simple: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اسْجُدُوا لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ قَالُوا وَمَا الرَّحْمَٰنُ أَنَسْجُدُ لِمَا تَأْمُرُنَا وَزَادَهُمْ نُفُورًا ۩

t.sahih-int: And when it is said to them, "Prostrate to the Most Merciful," they say, "And what is the Most Merciful? Should we prostrate to that which you order us?" And it increases them in aversion
t.asad: Yet when they [who are bent on denying the truth! are told, “Prostrate yourselves before the Most Gracious.” they are wont to ask, “And [who and] what is the Most Gracious? Are we to prostrate ourselves before whatever thou biddest us [to worship]?” - and so [thy call] but increases their aversion
